vocabulary.definition: n. 鉴赏的能力, 行家的地位
vocabulary.context: He prided himself on his connoisseurship in wine.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他对自己的葡萄酒鉴赏力感到自豪

vocabulary.definition: n. 姿势, 手势, 用姿势表达
vocabulary.context: He laughed and threw the bottle upwards with a gesticulation I did not understand.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他笑着把瓶子向上扔了出去，做了个我不明白的手势

vocabulary.definition: adv. 杂乱地, 混杂地
vocabulary.context: From the fourth side the bones had been thrown down, and lay promiscuously upon the earth, forming at one point a mound of some size.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 骨头从第四面被扔下来，乱七八糟地躺在地上，在某一处形成了一个一定大小的土堆

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不能忍受地
vocabulary.context: The vaults are insufferably damp.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 拱顶潮湿得令人难以忍受

vocabulary.definition: n. 地下墓穴, 地窖
vocabulary.context: We came at length to the foot of the descent, and stood together upon the damp ground of the catacombs of the Montresors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们终于来到了山坡脚下，并一起站在蒙特雷索地下墓穴潮湿的地面上

vocabulary.definition: n. 宰杀, 献身, 祭物
vocabulary.context: I continued, as was my wont to smile in his face, and he did not perceive that my smile now was at the thought of his immolation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我继续说道，就像我习惯在他脸上微笑一样，他没有意识到我现在的微笑是因为想到他的自焚

vocabulary.definition: adj. 紧身的
vocabulary.context: He had on a tight-fitting parti-striped dress, and his head was surmounted by the conical cap and bells.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他穿着一件紧身的条纹连衣裙，头上戴着圆锥形帽子和铃铛

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未纠正的, 未昭雪的, 未调整的, 未补偿(或补救)的
vocabulary.context: A wrong is unredressed when retribution overtakes its redresser.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当报应超过其纠正者时，错误就无法得到纠正

vocabulary.definition: n. 不学无术的人, 无知识的人；[法] 笨人, 毫无知识的人, 不予起诉
vocabulary.context: "He is an ignoramus," interrupted my friend, as he stepped unsteadily forward, while I followed immediately at his heels.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "他是个无知者。"我的朋友摇摇晃晃地向前走去，打断了他的话，而我则紧随其后

vocabulary.definition: n. 欺诈, 冒牌
vocabulary.context: For the most part their enthusiasm is adopted to suit the time and opportunity, to practise imposture upon the British and Austrian millionaires.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们的热情大多是为了适应时机和机会，对英国和奥地利的百万富翁进行欺骗

vocabulary.definition: n. 男仆, 僮仆
vocabulary.context: So, the year I turned eight, we got a new houseboy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所以，在我八岁那年，我们有了一个新男仆

vocabulary.definition: n. 酒椰叶纤维
vocabulary.context: Then one Saturday, we went to his village to visit, and his mother showed us a beautifully patterned basket made of dyed raffia that his brother had made.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后一个星期六，我们去他的村庄参观，他的母亲向我们展示了他哥哥用染色拉菲草制成的图案精美的篮子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 弯曲的, 古怪的
vocabulary.context: I realized that people like me, girls with skin the color of chocolate, whose kinky hair could not form ponytails, could also exist in literature.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我发现，像我这样皮肤巧克力色、头发卷成马尾辫的女孩，也可以存在于文学中

vocabulary.definition: n. 编穗带的人, 缝辫机, 穗带制作机；[化] 编织机
vocabulary.context: What if my roommate knew about my wonderfully ambitious hair braider, who has just started her own business selling hair extensions?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果我的室友知道我雄心勃勃的编发师，她刚刚开始自己的接发业务，该怎么办？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 和他人未婚同居的；n. 未婚同居的对象
vocabulary.context: And so we had, as was the norm, live-in domestic help, who would often come from nearby rural villages.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，按照惯例，我们有住家女佣，他们通常来自附近的乡村

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 赋予人性, 教化, 使通人情；v.i. 变为有人性, 变得有情, 有教化
vocabulary.context: Stories have been used to dispossess and to malign, but stories can also be used to empower and to humanize.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 故事被用来剥夺和诽谤，但故事也可以用来赋予权力和人性化

vocabulary.definition: n. 滥用者
vocabulary.context: I recently spoke at a university where a student told me that it was such a shame that Nigerian men were physical abusers like the father character in my novel.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我最近在一所大学演讲，一名学生告诉我，尼日利亚男人就像我小说中的父亲角色一样，是身体虐待者，这真是太耻辱了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有害的, 恶性的, 有恶意的；v.t. 诽谤, 说坏话
vocabulary.context: Stories have been used to dispossess and to malign, but stories can also be used to empower and to humanize.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 故事被用来剥夺和诽谤，但故事也可以用来赋予权力和人性化

vocabulary.definition: adj. 易感的, 敏感的, 可塑的
vocabulary.context: What this demonstrates, I think, is how impressionable and vulnerable we are in the face of a story, particularly as children.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我认为，这表明我们在面对故事时是多么容易受影响和脆弱，尤其是作为孩子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 愚昧无知的, 未受启发的
vocabulary.context: Socrates : And now, let me show in a figure how far our nature is enlightened or unenlightened: - Behold!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 苏格拉底：现在，让我用一个图形来展示我们的本性是开悟还是未开悟的程度： - 看哪！

vocabulary.definition: n. 牵线木偶
vocabulary.context: Above and behind them a fire is blazing at a distance, and between the fire and the prisoners there is a raised way; and you will see, if you look, a low wall built along the way, like the screen which marionette players have in front of them, over which they show the puppets.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他们的上方和后方，远处有一团火在熊熊燃烧，在火和囚犯之间有一条高高的道路；如果你看的话，你会看到沿途建起一堵矮墙，就像木偶演员面前的屏幕，他们在屏幕上展示木偶

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 误会, 误解
vocabulary.context: ...dear Glaucon, to the previous argument; the prison-house is the world of sight, the light of the fire is the sun, and you will not misapprehend me if you interpret the journey upwards to be the ascent of the soul into the intellectual world according to my poor belief, which, at your desire, I have expressed whether rightly or wrongly God knows.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...亲爱的格劳孔，对于前面的论点；监狱是视觉世界，火光是太阳，如果你根据我可怜的信念将向上的旅程解释为灵魂进入智力世界，你就不会误解我，我按照你的意愿表达了上帝知道的正确或错误

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 庆祝, 庆贺
vocabulary.context: Socrates : And when he remembered his old habitation, and the wisdom of the den and his fellow-prisoners, do you not suppose that he would felicitate himself on the change, and pity them?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 苏格拉底：当他想起他的旧居所、牢房的智慧和他的狱友时，你不认为他会为自己的变化感到庆幸并怜悯他们吗？

vocabulary.definition: adv. 理性地；讲道理地
vocabulary.context: ...world, and the immediate source of reason and truth in the intellectual; and that this is the power upon which he who would act rationally, either in public or private life, must have his eye fixed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...世界，以及知识分子中理性和真理的直接来源；无论是在公共生活中还是在私人生活中，任何一个想要理性行事的人都必须关注这种力量

vocabulary.definition: n. 上升, 提高, 攀登, 上坡路
vocabulary.context: Socrates : And suppose once more, that he is reluctantly dragged up a steep and rugged ascent, and held fast until he's forced into the presence of the sun himself, is he not likely to be pained and irritated?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 苏格拉底：再假设一次，他被不情愿地拖上陡峭崎岖的山坡，并被紧紧抓住，直到他被迫来到太阳面前，他难道不会感到痛苦和恼怒吗？

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 附加, 增补, 盖章；[计] DOS外部命令:为数据文件(非执行文件)设定一个或多个磁盘路径；当程序执行时, DOS将在所设定的磁盘路径中查找不在当前路径下的文件
vocabulary.context: Socrates : This entire allegory, you may now append, dear Glaucon, to the previous argument; the prison-house is the world of sight, the light of the fire is the sun, and you will not misapprehend me if you interpret the journey upwards to be the ascent...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 苏格拉底：亲爱的格劳孔，您现在可以将整个寓言附加到之前的论证中；监狱是视觉世界，火光是太阳，如果你把向上的旅程理解为上升，你就不会误解我了......

vocabulary.definition: n. 相反的事物, 倒, 逆向；adj. 相反的, 逆向的, 颠倒的；v.i. 交谈, 谈话
vocabulary.context: Socrates : And if they were able to converse with one another, would they not suppose that they were naming what was actually before them?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 苏格拉底：如果他们能够互相交谈，他们会不会认为他们正在说出他们面前实际存在的东西？

vocabulary.definition: n. 使失人性, 非人化, 灭绝人性, 使成兽性
vocabulary.context: The same social psychological processes - deindividualization, anonymity of place, dehumanization, role-playing and social modeling, moral disengagement, and group conformity - that acted in the Stanford Prison Experiment were at play at Abu Ghraib, Zimbardo argued.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 津巴多认为，斯坦福监狱实验中发生的相同社会心理过程--去个性化、地方匿名性、非人性化、角色扮演和社会模范、道德脱离和群体整合--在阿布格莱布监狱也发挥着作用

vocabulary.definition: n. 分开, 空闲, 解脱；[医] 解脱(分娩)
vocabulary.context: The same social psychological processes - deindividualization, anonymity of place, dehumanization, role-playing and social modeling, moral disengagement, and group conformity - that acted in the Stanford Prison Experiment were at play at Abu Ghraib, Zimbardo argued.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 津巴多认为，斯坦福监狱实验中发生的相同社会心理过程--去个性化、地方匿名性、非人性化、角色扮演和社会模范、道德脱离和群体整合--在阿布格莱布监狱也发挥着作用

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有渗透性的, 能透过的；[化] 渗透性的; 可渗透的
vocabulary.context: "That line between good and evil is permeable," Zimbardo said.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "善与恶之间的界限是可以渗透的，"津巴多说

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 切断, 使残废, 使不完整；[医] 致残毁
vocabulary.context: As it turned out, 80 percent of warriors in these cultures were found to be more destructive - for example, killing, torturing or mutilating their victims - than unpainted or unmasked warriors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实证明，这些文化中 80% 的战士比未涂漆或未戴面具的战士更具破坏性，例如杀害、折磨或残害受害者

vocabulary.definition: n. 为恶者, 做坏事的人
vocabulary.context: Instead, just the right dose of certain social situations can transform ordinarily good people into evildoers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 相反，某些社会情境的适当剂量可以将普通的好人变成坏人

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 脱去假面具；v.t. 暴露
vocabulary.context: As it turned out, 80 percent of warriors in these cultures were found to be more destructive - for example, killing, torturing or mutilating their victims - than unpainted or unmasked warriors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实证明，这些文化中 80% 的战士比未涂漆或未戴面具的战士更具破坏性，例如杀害、折磨或残害受害者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无复层的；未上漆的
vocabulary.context: As it turned out, 80 percent of warriors in these cultures were found to be more destructive - for example, killing, torturing or mutilating their victims - than unpainted or unmasked warriors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实证明，这些文化中 80% 的战士比未涂漆或未戴面具的战士更具破坏性，例如杀害、折磨或残害受害者

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使失人性, 非人化, 灭绝人性, 使成兽性
vocabulary.context: Particularly notable, Zimbardo said, is that people are seduced into evil by dehumanizing and labeling others.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 津巴多说，特别值得注意的是，人们通过非人化和给他人贴上标签而被引诱走向邪恶

vocabulary.definition: n. 忽视, 漠视；v.t. 忽视, 不顾
vocabulary.context: The experiment showed that institutional forces and peer pressure led normal student volunteer guards to disregard the potential harm of their actions on the other student prisoners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 实验表明，制度力量和同伴压力导致正常的学生志愿者看守无视他们的行为对其他学生囚犯的潜在伤害

vocabulary.definition: n. 不以为然, 非难
vocabulary.context: And the rumor of this new presence having spread itself whisperingly around, there arose at length from the whole company a buzz, or murmur, expressive of disapprobation and surprise - then, finally, of terror, of horror, and of disgust.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 关于这个新存在的谣言已经悄悄地传播开来，最后整个公司都发出了嗡嗡声或低语声，表达着不赞成和惊讶--然后，最后，是恐惧、恐怖和厌恶

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不间断地, 连续地
vocabulary.context: ...person; and, while the vast assembly, as if with one impulse, shrank from the centres of the rooms to the walls, he made his way uninterruptedly, but with the same solemn and measured step which had distinguished him from the first, through the blue chamber to the purple - through the purple to the green - through the green to the orange -...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ......人；当一大群人仿佛一时冲动从房间中央向墙壁退去时，他却不间断地前行，但步伐与第一个不同，他的步伐同样庄严而有节奏，穿过蓝色房间到紫色房间--从紫色房间到绿色房间--从绿色房间到橙色房间--......

vocabulary.definition: n. 枝状大烛台

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 现在，七间公寓中没有一间有任何一盏灯或烛台，周围散落着大量的金色装饰品，或从屋顶垂下来

vocabulary.definition: adj. 亵渎神明的, 不敬神的；[法] 侮辱性的, 诽谤性的
vocabulary.context: "Who dares?" he demanded hoarsely of the courtiers who stood near him - "who dares insult us with this blasphemous mockery?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "谁敢？"他嘶哑地向站在他身边的朝臣们问道："谁敢用这种亵渎的嘲弄来侮辱我们？

vocabulary.definition: n. 发抖;胆小
vocabulary.context: ...and six hundred seconds of the Time that flies,) there came yet another chiming of the clock, and then were the same disconcert and tremulousness and meditation as before.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ......时光飞逝，六百秒，）时钟又敲响了，然后是和以前一样的不安、颤抖和冥想

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但戏子竟然扮演了红死病的类型

vocabulary.definition: n. 城垛, 雉堞墙
vocabulary.context: Seize him and unmask him - that we may know whom we have to hang at sunrise, from the battlements!"
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 抓住他，揭开他的面具--这样我们就可以知道日出时我们必须在城垛上吊死谁！"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 造成城形的, 构造如城的, 有城的
vocabulary.context: When his dominions were half depopulated, he summoned to his presence a thousand hale and light-hearted friends from among the knights and dames of his court, and with these retired to the deep seclusion of one of his castellated abbeys.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当他的领土人口减少一半时，他从宫廷的骑士和贵妇中召集了一千名精神抖擞、心情愉快的朋友，并带着这些朋友隐退到他的一座城堡修道院的幽静之处

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 比...更暴虐,比......作得更过分
vocabulary.context: In truth the masquerade license of the night was nearly unlimited; but the figure in question had out-Heroded Herod, and gone beyond the bounds of even the prince's indefinite decorum.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，当晚的化装舞会几乎没有限制。但这个人物已经超越了希律王，甚至超越了王子无限礼仪的界限

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可允许的, 可容许程度的；[机] 容许, 允许

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一颗子弹贯穿始终，

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她不敢再用眼神询问他

vocabulary.definition: adv. 掌心向上地, 仰卧地

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们是否应该通过仰卧并拥抱希望的虚幻幻影来获得有效抵抗的手段，直到我们的敌人束缚我们的手脚？

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 英国在世界这一地区有敌人要求集结海军和陆军吗？

vocabulary.definition: v. 恳求, 哀求, 恳请
vocabulary.context: We 11 12 have petitioned, we have remonstrated, we have supplicated, we have prostrated ourselves their deaths 2.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们11 12曾请愿，我们曾抗议，我们曾恳求，我们曾屈服于他们的死亡2

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 举动, 表现；v.i. 相称, 适合
vocabulary.context: Comport(verb)to fit or agree 7.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 配合（动词）以适合或同意 7.

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 表示异议, 抗议, 责备；v.t. 抗议地说
vocabulary.context: We 11 12 have petitioned, we have remonstrated, we have supplicated, we have prostrated ourselves their deaths 2.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们11 12曾请愿，我们曾抗议，我们曾恳求，我们曾屈服于他们的死亡2

vocabulary.definition: adj. 困惑的, 迷惑的, 欺瞒的
vocabulary.context: Shall we acquire the means of effectual resistance by lying supinely on our backs, and hugging the delusive phantom of hope, until our enemies shall have bound us hand and foot?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们是否应该通过仰卧并拥抱希望的虚幻幻影来获得有效抵抗的手段，直到我们的敌人束缚我们的手脚？

vocabulary.definition: n. 弟子, 门徒
vocabulary.context: A biblical allusion to the betrayal of Jesus by his disciple Judas, who kisses Jesus at the moment of his betrayal.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 圣经中提到耶稣被他的门徒犹大背叛，犹大在耶稣被背叛的那一刻亲吻了耶稣

vocabulary.definition: n. 无决断, 优柔寡断；[法] 优柔寡断, 摇摆不定, 犹豫不决
vocabulary.context: Shall we gather strength by irresolution and inaction?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们是否应该通过优柔寡断和无所作为来积聚力量？

vocabulary.definition: n. 不可预测, 不可解, 不可思议
vocabulary.context: Secure, however, in the inscrutability of my place of concealment, I felt no embarrassment whatever.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，在我的隐藏地点的神秘莫测中，我并没有感到任何尴尬

vocabulary.definition: adj. 难管制的, 难压制的, 放肆的；[法] 难控制的, 难驾驭的, 难统治的
vocabulary.context: The moodiness of my usual temper increased to hatred of all things and of all mankind; while, from the sudden, frequent, and ungovernable outbursts of a fury to which I now blindly abandoned myself, my uncomplaining wife, alas!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我平日的喜怒无常变成了对万物、对全人类的仇恨；然而，由于突然、频繁、无法控制的愤怒爆发，我现在盲目地抛弃了自己，我那毫无怨言的妻子，唉！

vocabulary.definition: n. 倔强；堕落
vocabulary.context: And then came, as if to my final and irrevocable overthrow, the spirit of PERVERSENESS.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，执着的精神似乎最终不可挽回地推翻了我

vocabulary.definition: adv. 极好地；优异地
vocabulary.context: By the bye, gentlemen, this - this is a very well constructed house." (In the rabid desire to say something easily, I scarcely knew what I uttered at all.) - "I may say an excellently well constructed house.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 再见，先生们，这是一座建造得非常好的房子。"（由于强烈地想要轻松地说些什么，我几乎不知道自己说了什么。）-"我可以说是一座建造得非常好的房子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自我牺牲的, 舍己为人的；[法] 自我牺牲的, 不自私的, 献身的
vocabulary.context: There is something in the unselfish and self-sacrificing love of a brute, which goes directly to the heart of him who has had frequent occasion to test the paltry friendship and gossamer fidelity of mere Man.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 野兽的无私和自我牺牲的爱中含有某种东西，它直接触及那些经常有机会考验人类的微不足道的友谊和薄纱般的忠诚的人的内心

vocabulary.definition: adj. 志趣不相投的; 忤
vocabulary.context: I married early, and was happy to find in my wife a disposition not uncongenial with my own.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我很早就结婚了，很高兴发现我妻子的性情与我的性格不谋而合

vocabulary.definition: adv. 相连地, 结合地
vocabulary.context: ...anomalous and inhuman - a howl - a wailing shriek, half of horror and half of triumph, such as might have arisen only out of hell, conjointly from the throats of the damned in their agony and of the demons that exult in the damnation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...异常和不人道 - 一声嚎叫 - 一声哀嚎，一半是恐惧，一半是胜利，这种声音只能从地狱中发出，同时从痛苦中的被诅咒者和在诅咒中狂喜的恶魔的喉咙中发出

vocabulary.definition: n. guiltless的变形
vocabulary.context: I burned to say if but one word, by way of triumph, and to render doubly sure their assurance of my guiltlessness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我迫不及待地想以胜利的方式说一句话，并让他们更加确信我无罪

vocabulary.definition: adj. 没有怨言的, 不发牢骚的, 不诉苦的, 耐心的, 坚忍的
vocabulary.context: The moodiness of my usual temper increased to hatred of all things and of all mankind; while, from the sudden, frequent, and ungovernable outbursts of a fury to which I now blindly abandoned myself, my uncomplaining wife, alas!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我平日的喜怒无常变成了对万物、对全人类的仇恨；然而，由于突然、频繁、无法控制的愤怒爆发，我现在盲目地抛弃了自己，我那毫无怨言的妻子，唉！

vocabulary.definition: adv. 简洁地; 简洁地, 简便地
vocabulary.context: My immediate purpose is to place before the world, plainly, succinctly, and without comment, a series of mere household events.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的直接目的是向世人简单、简洁、不加评论地展示一系列纯粹的家庭事件

vocabulary.definition: n. 分离, 断开, 分开；[计] 断开, 拆接, 不连接
vocabulary.context: The increased risk of premature death associated with social disconnection is comparable to smoking daily - and may be even greater than the risk associated with obesity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与社交脱节相关的过早死亡风险增加与每天吸烟相当，甚至可能比与肥胖相关的风险更大

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 重新谈判
vocabulary.context: Second, we have to renegotiate our relationship with technology, creating space in our lives without our devices so we can be more present with one another.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其次，我们必须重新协商我们与技术的关系，在没有设备的情况下为我们的生活创造空间，这样我们就可以更多地彼此相处

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使适应, 再调整
vocabulary.context: It will require reorienting ourselves, our communities, and our institutions to prioritize human connection and healthy relationships.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这需要重新定位我们自己、我们的社区和我们的机构，优先考虑人际关系和健康的关系

vocabulary.definition: n. 极化, 极化强度, 偏振, 两极分化；[计] 定位; 对位
vocabulary.context: When we are less invested in one another, we are more susceptible to polarization and less able to pull together to face the challenges that we cannot solve alone - from climate change and gun violence to economic inequality and future pandemics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我们相互投资减少时，我们就更容易出现两极分化，也更不可能齐心协力应对我们无法单独解决的挑战--从气候变化和枪支暴力到经济不平等和未来的流行病

vocabulary.definition: adj. 隐伏的, 阴险的, 狡猾的；[医] 隐袭的
vocabulary.context: But its invisibility is part of what makes it so insidious.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但它的不可见性是其如此阴险的部分原因

vocabulary.definition: n. 享有, 保有期；[经] (财产,职位等的)占有, 占有权
vocabulary.context: It might not have been so bad had I not made a critical mistake: I had largely neglected my friendships during my tenure, convincing myself that I had to focus on work and I couldn't do both.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果我没有犯一个严重的错误，情况可能不会那么糟糕：在我任职期间，我很大程度上忽视了我的友谊，说服自己我必须专注于工作，而我不能两者兼顾

vocabulary.definition: adj. 早产的, 过早的, 不成熟的；n. 早产儿, 过早发生的事物
vocabulary.context: The increased risk of premature death associated with social disconnection is comparable to smoking daily - and may be even greater than the risk associated with obesity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与社交脱节相关的过早死亡风险增加与每天吸烟相当，甚至可能比与肥胖相关的风险更大

vocabulary.definition: adj. 集体的, 聚集的, 共同的；[医] 集体的, 集合的
vocabulary.context: We need to acknowledge the loneliness and isolation that millions are experiencing and the grave consequences for our mental health, physical health and collective well-being.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们需要承认数百万人正在经历的孤独和孤立，以及对我们的心理健康、身体健康和集体福祉的严重后果

vocabulary.definition: adv. 天文学上地；宇航学地；天体地
vocabulary.context: They were too God-intoxicated to be "astronomically intimidated." By their effort and example they brought an end to such ancient evils as infanticide and gladiatorial contests.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们对上帝太着迷了，不会被"天文吓倒"。通过他们的努力和榜样，他们结束了杀婴和角斗比赛等古老的罪恶

vocabulary.definition: n. <主美>主张取消种族隔离的人
vocabulary.context: I also hope that circumstances will soon make it possible for me to meet each of you, not as an integrationist or a civil-rights leader but as a fellow clergyman and a Christian brother.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我也希望不久的将来，我能够以一名牧师和基督教兄弟的身份，而不是作为一个融合主义者或民权领袖，与你们每一个人见面

vocabulary.definition: n. interrelated的变形
vocabulary.context: Moreover, I am cognizant of the interrelatedness of all communities and states.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，我认识到所有社区和国家的相互关联性

vocabulary.definition: adv. 在社会学上地
vocabulary.context: Hence segregation is not only politically, economically and sociologically unsound, it is morally wrong and sinful.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，种族隔离不仅在政治、经济和社会学上是不健全的，而且在道德上也是错误和有罪的

vocabulary.definition: adv. 建设性地
vocabulary.context: Actually, time itself is neutral; it can be used either destructively or constructively.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，时间本身是中性的；它可以被用来破坏性地或建设性地使用

vocabulary.definition: adv. 非暴力地
vocabulary.context: In this sense they have conducted themselves rather "nonviolently" in public.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从这个意义上说，他们在公共场合的行为相当"非暴力"

vocabulary.definition: adv. 破坏地；狼狈地
vocabulary.context: Actually, time itself is neutral; it can be used either destructively or constructively.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，时间本身是中性的；它可以被用来破坏性地或建设性地使用

vocabulary.definition: adj. 假装圣洁的, 假装虔诚的, 假装诚实的
vocabulary.context: ...blatant injustices inflicted upon the Negro, I have watched white churchmen stand on the sideline and mouth pious irrelevancies and sanctimonious trivialities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...对黑人的公然不公正，我看到白人牧师站在一旁，口中说出虔诚的无关紧要的话和道貌岸然的琐事

vocabulary.definition: v. 发出火花, 闪耀光芒
vocabulary.context: Let us all hope that the dark clouds of racial prejudice will soon pass away and the deep fog of misunderstanding will be lifted from our fear drenched communities, and in some not too distant tomorrow the radiant stars of love and brotherhood will shine over our great nation with all their scintillating beauty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 让我们大家都希望，种族偏见的乌云很快就会散去，误解的浓雾将从我们充满恐惧的社区中散去，在不远的明天，爱和兄弟情谊的光芒四射的星星将以其所有闪烁的美丽照耀我们伟大的国家

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不合语法的, 不正确的
vocabulary.context: ...Alabama, who rose up with a sense of dignity and with her people decided not to ride segregated buses, and who responded with ungrammatical profundity to one who inquired about her weariness: "My feets is tired, but my soul is at rest." They will be the young high school and college students, the young ministers of the gospel and a host of...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...阿拉巴马州带着尊严感站起来，与她的人民一起决定不乘坐种族隔离的公共汽车，当有人询问她的疲倦时，她以不合语法的深刻方式回答："我的脚很累，但我的灵魂在休息。"他们将是年轻的高中生和大学生、年轻的福音传道人和许多......

vocabulary.definition: n. 技工, 发明者；[医] 牙技工
vocabulary.context: soft fine fluffy feathers, typically referring to birds' feathers When he had put the last touches to what he had begun, the artificer balanced his own body between the two wings and hovered in the moving air.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 柔软细腻的蓬松羽毛，通常指的是鸟类的羽毛当他完成了他开始的最后一步时，技师在两个翅膀之间平衡了自己的身体，并在移动的空气中盘旋

vocabulary.definition: n. 把犁人, 庄稼汉
vocabulary.context: Some angler catching fish with a quivering rod, or a shepherd leaning on his crook, or a ploughman resting on the handles of his plough, saw them, perhaps, and stood there amazed, believing them to be gods able to travel the sky.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 某个用颤抖的鱼竿钓鱼的钓鱼者，或者一个拄着拐杖的牧羊人，或者一个靠在犁柄上的农夫，也许看到了他们，并惊讶地站在那里，相信他们是能够在天空中旅行的神

vocabulary.definition: n. 年龄, 老年, 成年, 寿命, 时代, 时期；v. 变老, 成熟
vocabulary.context: While he worked and issued his warnings the ageing man's cheeks were wet with tears: the father's hands trembled.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当他工作并发出警告时，老人的脸颊被泪水浸湿：父亲的手在颤抖

vocabulary.definition: n. 牧人, 牧主
vocabulary.context: And I order you not to aim towards Bootes, the Herdsman, or Helice, the Great Bear, or towards the drawn sword of Orion: take the course I show you!' At the same time as he laid down the rules of flight, he fitted the newly created wings on the boy's shoulders.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我命令你不要瞄准牧人布提斯、大熊赫利斯或猎户座拔出的剑：走我向你展示的路线！在制定飞行规则的同时，他将新制作的翅膀安装在男孩的肩膀上

vocabulary.definition: n. 接近；密切；近似
vocabulary.context: His nearness to the devouring sun softened the fragrant wax that held the wings: and the wax melted: he flailed with bare arms, but losing his oar-like wings, could not ride the air.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他靠近吞噬太阳的地方，软化了翅膀上芬芳的蜡：蜡融化了：他赤裸地挥舞着手臂，但失去了桨般的翅膀，无法在空中飞行

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 弯曲, 伸缩, 折曲；v.i. 弯曲；n. 屈曲, 折曲
vocabulary.context: Then he fastened them together with thread at the middle, and beeswax at the base, and, when he had arranged them, he flexed each one into a gentle curve, so that they imitated real bird's wings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然后，他用中间的线和底部的蜂蜡将它们固定在一起，当他排列好它们后，他将它们弯曲成柔和的曲线，使它们模仿真正的鸟的翅膀

vocabulary.definition: adj. 奇异的, 神奇的, 奇迹般的, 惊人的, 不可思议的, 绝妙的, 妙极的
vocabulary.context: His son, Icarus, stood next to him, and, not realising that he was handling things that would endanger him, caught laughingly at the down that blew in the passing breeze, and softened the yellow beeswax with his thumb, and, in his play, hindered his father's marvellous work.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的儿子伊卡洛斯站在他旁边，没有意识到他正在处理的事情会危及他，他笑着抓住了微风吹过的羽绒，并用拇指软化了黄色蜂蜡，在他的玩耍中，阻碍了他父亲的奇妙工作

vocabulary.definition: n. 乡下人, 乡巴佬；adj. 乡村的, 纯朴的, 手工粗糙的
vocabulary.context: In that way, long ago, the rustic pan-pipes were graduated, with lengthening reeds.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就这样，很久以前，质朴的排笛就被分级了，簧片也加长了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 横放的；v.t. 反对, 阻挠, 横过；prep. 横过；adv. 横过
vocabulary.context: 'He may thwart our escape by land or sea' he said 'but the sky is surely open to us: we will go that way: Minos rules everything but he does not rule the heavens.' So saying he applied his thought to new invention and altered the natural order of things.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "他可能会阻止我们通过陆地或海上逃跑，"他说，"但天空肯定向我们敞开：我们会走那条路：米诺斯统治一切，但他不统治天堂。"这么说，他将自己的思想应用于新发明并改变了事物的自然秩序

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不腐败的, 不能收买的, 清廉的
vocabulary.context: This vast amphitheater, with its encircling galleries, its mysterious vaults, and its unseen passages, was an agent of poetic justice, in which crime was punished, or virtue rewarded, by the decrees of an impartial and incorruptible chance.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个巨大的圆形剧场，及其环绕的画廊、神秘的拱顶和看不见的通道，是诗意正义的代理人，在这里，犯罪受到惩罚，美德得到奖励，通过公正和廉洁的机会的法令

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不受限制的, 自由自在的, 不受妨碍的
vocabulary.context: In the very olden time there lived a semi-barbaric king, whose ideas, though somewhat polished and sharpened by the progressiveness of distant Latin neighbors, were still large, florid, and untrammeled, as became the half of him which was barbaric.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在非常古老的时代，有一位半野蛮的国王，他的思想虽然因遥远的拉丁邻国的进步而有所打磨和锐化，但仍然是宏大、华丽和不受约束的，就像他野蛮的一半一样

vocabulary.definition: adj. 兴高采烈的, 狂喜的, 欢天喜地的
vocabulary.context: How in her grievous reveries had she gnashed her teeth, and torn her hair, when she saw his start of rapturous delight as he opened the door of the lady!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当她看到他打开女士的门时欣喜若狂的时候，她是多么在悲痛的遐想中咬牙切齿，撕扯着自己的头发！

vocabulary.definition: n. 将来, 未来, 未来的事, 远景
vocabulary.context: Would it not be better for him to die at once, and go to wait for her in the blessed regions of semi-barbaric futurity?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果他立即死去，然后去半野蛮未来的福地等她，这不是更好吗？

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 隆重地庆祝, 庄重举行, 使庄严
vocabulary.context: Another door opened beneath the king, and a priest, followed by a band of choristers, and dancing maidens blowing joyous airs on golden horns and treading an epithalamic measure, advanced to where the pair stood, side by side, and the wedding was promptly and cheerily solemnized.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一扇门在国王下方打开，一位牧师，后面跟着一群唱诗班歌手，以及吹着金角、踩着上丘节的舞女，走到两人并肩站立的地方，婚礼迅速而愉快地隆重举行

vocabulary.definition: v. 接枝, 灌输
vocabulary.context: ...brain of this man, who, every barleycorn a king, knew no tradition to which he owed more allegiance than pleased his fancy, and who ingrafted on every adopted form of human thought and action the rich growth of his barbaric idealism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...这个人的大脑，每个大麦粒都是一个国王，他知道没有什么传统是他应该效忠的，而不是满足他的幻想的，并且他将他野蛮的理想主义的丰富成长嫁接到人类思想和行动的每一种形式上

vocabulary.definition: adj. 确定的, 坚决的, 决定的；[法] 决定性的, 限定性的, 有限的
vocabulary.context: The decisions of this tribunal were not only fair, they were positively determinate: the accused person was instantly punished if he found himself guilty, and, if innocent, he was rewarded on the spot, whether he liked it or not.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个法庭的裁决不仅是公平的，而且是明确的：被告如果发现自己有罪，就会立即受到惩罚；如果被告无罪，无论他喜欢与否，他都会当场得到奖励

vocabulary.definition: adj. 当之无愧的，罪有应得的
vocabulary.context: ...notice was given that on an appointed day the fate of the accused person would be decided in the king's arena, a structure which well-deserved its name, for, although its form and plan were borrowed from afar, its purpose emanated solely from the brain of this man, who, every barleycorn a king, knew no tradition to which he owed more allegiance...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...通知指出，在指定的日子，被告的命运将在国王竞技场中决定，这是一个名副其实的建筑，因为尽管其形式和计划是从远方借来的，但其目的完全来自这个人的大脑，他，每一个大麦粒都是国王，不知道他应该更效忠的传统......

vocabulary.definition: n. 响亮持久的声音, 钟声；v.t. 使鸣响, 大声发出；v.i. 大声鸣响, 震响

vocabulary.definition: adj. 多数的, 拥挤的, 种种的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 大萧条始于 1929 年，发展平平无奇

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不笑的, 面无笑容的, 严肃的
vocabulary.context: Bareheaded and unsmiling, he focused his inaugural address on the parlous situation of the country and pledged to restore it to health:
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他光着头，面无笑容，在就职演说中重点讨论了该国的危险局势，并承诺让其恢复健康：

vocabulary.definition: adj. 危险的, 不易对付的, 精明的, 麻烦的；adv. 非常地, 极度地
vocabulary.context: Bareheaded and unsmiling, he focused his inaugural address on the parlous situation of the country and pledged to restore it to health:

vocabulary.definition: adj. 极右派的, 极端保守主义的
vocabulary.context: In France, the depression triggered street riots and strained the nation's social fabric by strengthening both socialists and their far-right opponents.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在法国，大萧条引发了街头骚乱，并通过加强社会主义者及其极右翼对手的力量而使国家的社会结构紧张

vocabulary.definition: n. (证券的)跌价
vocabulary.context: On October 24, 1929, a day remembered as "Black Thursday," enough investors decided to cash in on the boom to start a massive sell-off that pricked the stock market bubble.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1929 年 10 月 24 日，这一天被称为"黑色星期四"，足够多的投资者决定利用繁荣获利，开始大规模抛售，刺破了股市泡沫

vocabulary.definition: n. 伦理学, 人种学；[医] 人种学
vocabulary.context: As the leader of the Democratic Party, he built a coalition of labor unions, big city machines, white ethnics, African Americans, and rural white Southerners.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为民主党领袖，他建立了一个由工会、大城市机器、白人、非裔美国人和南方农村白人组成的联盟

vocabulary.definition: adv. 嘲笑地, 嘲弄地
vocabulary.context: Hundreds of thousands of Americans had lost their homes and lived in tents and shantytowns derisively called "Hoovervilles."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 数十万美国人失去了家园，住在被嘲笑为"胡佛村"的帐篷和棚户区里

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有钱的；[法] 金钱上的, 有钱的, 富有的
vocabulary.context: Roosevelt's support for labor unions and regulations for business and high finance earned him the enmity of many in the moneyed circles, but in FDR: An Intimate History , biographer Nathan Miller describes the president as unaffected by these critics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 罗斯福对工会以及商业和高级金融法规的支持为他招致了许多富人的敌意，但在《罗斯福：一段亲密的历史》中，传记作家内森·米勒形容总统并未受到这些批评的影响

vocabulary.definition: n. 节省...的器具, 节约装置, 省俭的人；[化] 回收器; 收集器
vocabulary.context: The Federal Deposit Insurance protected savers from losing their money in a bank crash.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 联邦存款保险保护储户免于在银行崩溃中损失金钱

vocabulary.definition: adj. 综合的, 一体化的
vocabulary.context: Meaning : An integrative memory is one in which you make meaning out of an event (such as growing emotionally following the death of a relative).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 意义：整合记忆是一种让你从事件中理解意义的记忆（例如在亲戚去世后情绪上的成长）

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非特异性的；[医] 非特异性的
vocabulary.context: A nonspecific memory describes a lengthy episode (such as the prolonged illness of a relative).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 非特异性记忆描述了一段漫长的经历（例如亲戚长期患病）

vocabulary.definition: n. 特异性, 专一性, 特殊性, 特性, 特征；[化] 专一性
vocabulary.context: Specificity : A highly specific memory refers to one event that had a relatively brief duration (such as a particularly enjoyable evening with friends).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 特异性：高度具体的记忆是指持续时间相对较短的事件（例如与朋友度过的一个特别愉快的夜晚）

vocabulary.definition: n. 健康, 幸福, 福利, 舒适, 安宁
vocabulary.context: In fact, many studies that look at these so-called "narratives" that people construct out of their lives suggest that it's not the event, but the meaning you make out of the event, that affects your sense of wellbeing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，许多研究人们在生活中构建的所谓"叙事"表明，影响你幸福感的不是事件，而是你从事件中理解的意义

vocabulary.definition: n. 自制
vocabulary.context: Four Dimensions of Self-Defining Memories (Specificity, Meaning, Content, and Affect) and Their Relationships to Self-Restraint, Distress, and Repressive Defensiveness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自我定义记忆的四个维度（特异性、意义、内容和情感）及其与自我克制、苦恼和压抑防御的关系

vocabulary.definition: n. 试验；adj. 试验性的, 暂时的
vocabulary.context: By the time we reach adolescence, we should have carved out at least a tentative sense of identity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我们进入青春期时，我们应该至少已经形成了一种尝试性的认同感

vocabulary.definition: n. 轶事, 奇闻
vocabulary.context: As the conversation unfolds, you'll probably elaborate with a few anecdotes that illustrate these facts about yourself and your life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着对话的展开，您可能会详细阐述一些轶事，以说明有关您自己和您的生活的这些事实

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无疑, 必定, 确实地
vocabulary.context: You are able to have a sense of identity because you know that you are the same person you were yesterday and will undoubtedly be the same person tomorrow.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你能够有一种认同感，因为你知道你还是昨天的你，明天也毫无疑问仍然是同一个人

vocabulary.context: The cornice but a mound.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 飞檐不过一座土丘

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我首先猜测是马头

vocabulary.context: The cornice but a mound.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 飞檐不过一座土丘

vocabulary.context: He kindly stopped for me;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他好心地为我停下来；

vocabulary.context: A swelling of the ground;
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 地面隆起；

vocabulary.definition: adj. 看得见的, 明显的, 显然的；n. 可见物
vocabulary.context: The roof was scarcely visible,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 屋顶几乎看不见，

vocabulary.definition: v. 使怨恨, 激怒( embitter的过去式和过去分词 )
vocabulary.context: A famous law suit, in the days of his grandfather, had wrested it from the illegal possession of a neighbouring family of petty landowners; the dispossessed party had never acquiesced in the judgment of the Courts, and a long series of poaching affrays and similar scandals had embittered the relationships between the families for three generations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他祖父时代，一场著名的诉讼从邻近的小地主家庭的非法占有中夺回了它。被剥夺者从来没有默许法院的判决，而一系列的偷猎斗殴和类似的丑闻已经使这个家族三代人之间的关系变得紧张

vocabulary.definition: n. 闯入者, 好事者, 无执照营业者；[经] 无执照营业者, 私商
vocabulary.context: We fight this quarrel out to the death, you and I and our foresters, with no cursed interlopers to come between us.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 你、我和我们的护林员，我们将这场争吵战斗到底，没有任何被诅咒的闯入者介入我们之间

vocabulary.definition: adj. 四足的
vocabulary.context: ...on the other, and this wind-scourged winter night Ulrich had banded together his foresters to watch the dark forest, not in quest of four-footed quarry, but to keep a look-out for the prowling thieves whom he suspected of being afoot from across the land boundary.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...另一方面，在这个狂风肆虐的冬夜，乌尔里希召集了他的林务人员来监视黑暗的森林，不是为了寻找四英尺的猎物，而是为了提防那些潜行的盗贼，他怀疑这些盗贼正从陆地边界上走来

vocabulary.definition: n. 边缘, 郊外, 外围
vocabulary.context: The forest lands of Gradwitz were of wide extent and well stocked with game; the narrow strip of precipitous woodland that lay on its outskirt was not remarkable for the game it harboured or the shooting it afforded, but it was the most jealously guarded of all its owner's territorial possessions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 格拉德维茨的林地面积广阔，猎物丰富；位于其郊区的那片狭窄的陡峭林地对于其栖息的游戏或提供的射击而言并不引人注目，但它是其所有者的所有领土财产中最受到严密保护的

vocabulary.definition: n. 尖叫声, 风笛声音；v.i. 发出尖锐声；v.t. 用风笛演奏
vocabulary.context: He strayed away by himself from the watchers whom he had placed in ambush on the crest of the hill, and wandered far down the steep slopes amid the wild tangle of undergrowth, peering through the tree trunks and listening through the whistling and skirling of the wind and the restless beating of the branches for sight and sound of the marauders.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他独自离开了在山顶埋伏的守望者，在茂密的灌木丛中沿着陡峭的山坡走得很远，透过树干凝视，通过风的呼啸声和树枝不停的拍打声来寻找掠夺者的视线和声音

vocabulary.definition: n. 猎鸟
vocabulary.context: I would never fire a shot on your land, save when you invited me as a guest; and you should come and shoot with me down in the marshes where the wildfowl are.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我绝不会在你的土地上开枪，除非你邀请我做客；你应该跟我一起去野鸟出没的沼泽地里射击

vocabulary.definition: n. 吵架, 滋事；[法] 吵嘴, 打架, 闹事
vocabulary.context: A famous law suit, in the days of his grandfather, had wrested it from the illegal possession of a neighbouring family of petty landowners; the dispossessed party had never acquiesced in the judgment of the Courts, and a long series of poaching affrays and similar scandals had embittered the relationships between the families for three generations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在他祖父时代，一场著名的诉讼从邻近的小地主家庭的非法占有中夺回了它。被剥夺者从来没有默许法院的判决，而一系列的偷猎斗殴和类似的丑闻已经使这个家族三代人之间的关系变得紧张

vocabulary.definition: n. 雄狍
vocabulary.context: The roebuck, which usually kept in the sheltered hollows during a storm-wind, were running like driven things to-night, and there was movement and unrest among the creatures that were wont to sleep through the dark hours.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 狍子通常在暴风雨期间待在有遮蔽的洞穴里，但今晚却像被驱赶的东西一样奔跑，习惯于在黑暗的时间里睡觉的生物之间出现了动静和不安

vocabulary.definition: n. 掳掠者, 攫食的动物, 抢劫者；[法] 抢劫者, 掳掠者
vocabulary.context: He strayed away by himself from the watchers whom he had placed in ambush on the crest of the hill, and wandered far down the steep slopes amid the wild tangle of undergrowth, peering through the tree trunks and listening through the whistling and skirling of the wind and the restless beating of the branches for sight and sound of the marauders.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他独自离开了在山顶埋伏的守望者，在茂密的灌木丛中沿着陡峭的山坡走得很远，透过树干凝视，通过风的呼啸声和树枝不停的拍打声来寻找掠夺者的视线和声音

vocabulary.definition: n. 继承人, 后继者；[法] 遗产继承人, 继承人
vocabulary.context: The neighbour feud had grown into a personal one since Ulrich had come to be head of his family; if there was a man in the world whom he detested and wished ill to it was Georg Znaeym, the inheritor of the quarrel and the tireless game-snatcher and raider of the disputed border-forest.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 自从乌尔里希成为一家之主后，邻居之间的恩怨就演变成了个人恩怨。如果说世界上有一个人让他憎恶并希望对他怀有恶意的话，那就是格奥尔格·兹奈姆（Georg Znaeym），他是争吵的继承者，也是有争议的边境森林的不知疲倦的猎物掠夺者和掠夺者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可消耗的；n. 消耗品
vocabulary.context: Style became cheap, convenient and consumable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 风格变得廉价、方便、易消耗

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 定量, 用数量表示, 测量
vocabulary.context: Though fast fashion represents an understandably alluring combination of style and savings, now, more than ever, we simply cannot quantify the true cost of our clothing with a price tag.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管快时尚代表了一种可以理解的时尚与节省的诱人结合，但现在，我们比以往任何时候都更无法用价格标签来量化服装的真实成本

vocabulary.definition: adv. 支撑得住；能保持住地
vocabulary.context: When consumers want to update their wardrobe, they can do so sustainably by thrifting, reworking old garments or researching environmentally-conscious brands.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当消费者想要更新他们的衣柜时，他们可以通过节俭、改造旧衣服或研究环保品牌来可持续地实现这一目标

vocabulary.definition: adj. 天蓝色的；n. 蓝宝石, 刚玉宝石, 天蓝色
vocabulary.context: After much deliberation, she settles on a glamorous sapphire gown with pleated details lining the bodice.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 经过深思熟虑，她选择了一件迷人的蓝宝石礼服，衣身衬里有褶皱细节

vocabulary.definition: adj. 随潮流的, 时髦的, 赶潮流的
vocabulary.context: During the 1990s, retailers began to introduce trendy, cheaply-priced, poorly-made clothes on a weekly basis, intending to match the breakneck pace at which fashion trends move.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在 20 世纪 90 年代，零售商开始每周推出流行、价格便宜、做工粗糙的服装，旨在跟上时尚潮流的飞速发展

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无法抵抗地, 不能自持地; 极为诱惑人地
vocabulary.context: Meanwhile, a reliance on cheap overseas labor and synthetic textiles keeps prices irresistibly low.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与此同时，对廉价海外劳动力和合成纺织品的依赖使价格保持在不可抗拒的低位

vocabulary.definition: n. 聚酯；[化] 聚酯
vocabulary.context: Virtually all of these brands sell clothes that contain petroleum-based, resource-intensive synthetic fibers such as polyester and nylon.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，所有这些品牌销售的衣服都含有基于石油的资源密集型合成纤维，如聚酯和尼龙

vocabulary.definition: adv. 可懂, 可了解, 可理解
vocabulary.context: Though fast fashion represents an understandably alluring combination of style and savings, now, more than ever, we simply cannot quantify the true cost of our clothing with a price tag.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管快时尚代表了一种可以理解的时尚与节省的诱人结合，但现在，我们比以往任何时候都更无法用价格标签来量化服装的真实成本

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 选择
vocabulary.context: During the pandemic, as stores closed, consumers ditched fast fashion staples such as H&M and Zara, instead opting to order from e-commerce social media sensations such as Shein and Asos.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 疫情期间，随着商店关门，消费者放弃了 H&M 和 Zara 等快时尚主打商品，转而选择从 Shein 和 Asos 等社交媒体热门电商网站订购

vocabulary.definition: adv. 欺骗地
vocabulary.context: ...and religious degradation,-in view of the unjust laws above mentioned, and because women do feel themselves aggrieved, oppressed, and fraudulently deprived of their most sacred rights, we insist that they have immediate admission to all the rights and privileges which belong to them as citizens of these United States.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...和宗教退化，鉴于上述不公正的法律，并且因为妇女确实感到自己受到了委屈、压迫，并被欺诈性地剥夺了她们最神圣的权利，我们坚持认为她们应立即承认她们作为美国公民应享有的所有权利和特权

vocabulary.definition: n. 媒介, 手段, 机构；[法] 媒介, 工具, 手段
vocabulary.context: In entering upon the great work before us, we anticipate no small amount of misconception, misrepresentation, and ridicule; but we shall use every instrumentality within our power to effect our object.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在着手开展我们面前的伟大工作时，我们预计会出现不少误解、曲解和嘲笑；但我们将利用我们力所能及的一切手段来实现我们的目标

vocabulary.definition: n. 报酬；[经] 报酬, 酬劳
vocabulary.context: He has monopolized nearly all the profitable employments, and from those she is permitted to follow, she receives but a scanty remuneration.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他几乎垄断了所有有利可图的工作，而她被允许从事的工作却只得到微薄的报酬

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可容忍的, 可忍耐的
vocabulary.context: Prudence, indeed, will dictate that governments long established should not be changed for light and transient causes; and accordingly, all experience hath shown that mankind are more disposed to suffer, while evils are sufferable, than to right themselves by abolishing the forms to which they are accustomed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，出于谨慎考虑，长期建立的政府不应因为轻微和短暂的原因而改变；因此，所有的经验都表明，当邪恶是可以忍受的时候，人类更愿意受苦，而不是通过废除他们所习惯的形式来纠正自己

vocabulary.definition: n. 惩罚；[经] 忝罚权
vocabulary.context: In the covenant of marriage, she is compelled to promise obedience to her husband, he becoming, to all intents and purposes, her master-the law giving him power to deprive her of her liberty, and to administer chastisement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在婚姻盟约中，她被迫承诺服从她的丈夫，他从所有意图和目的上都成为她的主人--法律赋予他剥夺她自由并实施惩罚的权力

vocabulary.definition: n. 容许, 默认, 默许；[法] 容许, 容忍
vocabulary.context: Such has been the patient sufferance of the women under this government, and such is now the necessity which constrains them to demand the equal station to which they are entitled.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这就是本届政府领导下妇女们所忍受的耐心，而现在这也是迫使她们要求她们应有的平等地位的必要性

vocabulary.definition: n. 监护, 保护, 守护；[法] 监护, 保护, 监护人的职责
vocabulary.context: He has so framed the laws of divorce, as to what shall be the proper causes of divorce; in case of separation, to whom the guardianship of the children shall be given; as to be wholly regardless of the happiness of women-the law, in all cases, going upon the false supposition of the supremacy of man, and giving all power into his hands.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他制定了离婚法，规定了离婚的正当理由；如果分居，子女的监护权应由谁承担；法律完全不考虑妇女的幸福--在所有情况下，法律都建立在男人至上的错误假设之上，并将所有权力交到男人手中

vocabulary.definition: adj. 受委屈的, 权利受到不法侵害的
vocabulary.context: ...country, their social and religious degradation,-in view of the unjust laws above mentioned, and because women do feel themselves aggrieved, oppressed, and fraudulently deprived of their most sacred rights, we insist that they have immediate admission to all the rights and privileges which belong to them as citizens of these United States.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...国家，她们的社会和宗教退化，鉴于上述不公正的法律，并且由于妇女确实感到自己受到委屈、压迫和被欺骗地剥夺了她们最神圣的权利，我们坚持认为她们应立即承认她们作为美国公民应享有的所有权利和特权

vocabulary.definition: n. 独裁, 专制, 专制政治；[法] 专制政体, 专制政治
vocabulary.context: But when a long train of abuses and usurpations, pursuing invariably the same object, evinces a design to reduce them under absolute despotism, it is their duty to throw off such government, and to provide new guards for their future security.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，当一长串的滥用职权和篡夺行为，总是追求同一个目标，显示出一种在绝对专制主义下减少这些行为的设计时，他们就有责任摆脱这样的政府，并为他们未来的安全提供新的警卫

vocabulary.definition: n. 特权, 君权, 职权；adj. 特权的, 行使特权的
vocabulary.context: He has usurped the prerogative of Jehovah himself, claiming it as his right to assign for her a sphere of action, when that belongs to her conscience and her God.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他篡夺了耶和华本人的特权，声称自己有权为她指定一个行动范围，而这实际上属于她的良心和上帝

vocabulary.definition: adv. 喧闹地, 吵闹地
vocabulary.context: The men laughed boisterously behind Joe's back as they watched him shamble woodward.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当人们看着乔摇摇晃晃地走向树林时，他们在背后大声笑了起来

vocabulary.definition: adj. 圆肩的
vocabulary.context: A round-shouldered figure in overalls much too large, came nervously in the door and the talking ceased.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个身穿大号工装裤的圆肩人物紧张地走进门，谈话停止了

vocabulary.definition: adv. 责难地
vocabulary.context: The men glared at Elijah, accusingly.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 男人们指责地瞪着伊利亚

vocabulary.definition: n. 开玩笑；嘲弄, 揶揄
vocabulary.context: No badinage this time.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这次没有恶作剧

vocabulary.definition: adj. 易惊的, 轻佻的, 三心二意的, 不可靠的, 羞怯的, 活泼的, 爱蹦跳的, 活跃的
vocabulary.context: Tell yuh the truth, Ah'm a lil bit skittish.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 说实话，我有点紧张

vocabulary.definition: n. 蚀刻工
vocabulary.context: He'd beat you full of button holes as quick as he's look etcher."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他会以最快的速度把你打得满是扣眼。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 轻快的, 潇洒的, 放荡的
vocabulary.context: The men in the store heard the sharp report of a pistol somewhere distant in the palmetto thicket and soon Spunk came walking leisurely, with his big black Stetson set at the same rakish angle and Lena clinging to his arm, came walking right into the general store.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 店里的人听到远处棕榈树丛中传来手枪的尖锐声响，很快斯邦克悠闲地走过来，他的黑色大斯泰森毡帽也摆成同样潇洒的角度，莉娜紧抓着他的手臂，径直走进了杂货店

vocabulary.definition: n. 悬挂者(物), 袜吊, 吊裤带；[医] 悬吊带, 悬吊器
vocabulary.context: He didn't bang it, just eased it out of his hand silently and fiddled with his suspender buckle.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他没有敲打它，只是默默地将它从手中松开，摆弄着他的背带扣

vocabulary.definition: n. 小门, 腰门, 售票窗
vocabulary.context: Spunk died too wicket - died cussin' he did.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 斯潘克死得太小门了--死的时候还咒骂着他死了

vocabulary.definition: n. 络纱工, 络筒机, 络纱机, 开发条的钥匙；[计] 卷线机, 绕线机
vocabulary.context: ...all over, you hear me, black, walked round and round that house and howled like forty, an' when Spunk got his gun an' went to the winder to shoot it he says it stood right still an' looked him in the eye, an' howled right at him.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ......一切都结束了，你听到我的声音，黑人，绕着房子走来走去，像四十岁一样嚎叫，当斯彭克拿起枪，走向发条器射击时，他说枪站着不动，看着他的眼睛，对着他嚎叫

vocabulary.definition: n. 使用手头现成工具摆弄修理, 利用手头东西制成的物品
vocabulary.context: Psychologists follow the lead of French anthropologist Claude Levi-Strauss in calling this skill bricolage.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 心理学家追随法国人类学家克洛德·列维-斯特劳斯的脚步，称这种技能为"拼凑"

vocabulary.definition: n. 不受欢迎, 失人心
vocabulary.context: Consider Phillip Morris, which has demonstrated impressive resilience in the face of increasing unpopularity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 以菲利普·莫里斯公司为例，该公司在面对日益不受欢迎的情况时表现出了令人印象深刻的韧性

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未确诊的,尚未找出原因的
vocabulary.context: I have a friend I'll call Jackie Oiseaux who suffered repeated psychoses over a 10-year period due to an undiagnosed bipolar disorder.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我有一个朋友，我称之为 Jackie Oiseaux，由于未确诊的双相情感障碍，他在 10 年内反复遭受精神病困扰

vocabulary.definition: adj. 实际的
vocabulary.context: Like Stockdale, resilient people have very sober and down-to-earth views of those parts of reality that matter for survival.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 像斯托克代尔一样，适应力强的人对现实中与生存至关重要的部分有着非常清醒和务实的看法

vocabulary.definition: n. 脚手架, 台架, (搭脚手架用的)支杆和木板, (喻)(论点等的)支柱；[化] 搭脚手架
vocabulary.context: Value systems at resilient companies change very little over the years and are used as scaffolding in times of trouble.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 弹性公司的价值体系多年来几乎没有变化，在遇到困难时被用作脚手架

vocabulary.definition: adj. 外来的, 非必要的, 无关的；[医] 体外的
vocabulary.context: Intriguingly, the roots of that word are closely tied to the concept of resilience, which literally means "bouncing back." Says Levi-Strauss: "In its old sense, the verb bricoler...was always used with reference to some extraneous movement: a ball rebounding, a dog straying, or a horse swerving from its direct course to avoid an obstacle."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有趣的是，这个词的根源与弹性的概念密切相关，弹性的字面意思是"反弹"。列维-斯特劳斯说："在过去的意义上，动词 bricoler......总是用来指代一些无关的运动：球反弹、狗迷路或马偏离其直接路线以避开障碍物。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 用力打击的, 能用力打击的
vocabulary.context: He churned out hard-hitting cover stories and features with a speed and elegance I could only dream of.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他以我梦寐以求的速度和优雅制作了强有力的封面故事和特写

vocabulary.definition: n. 撬窃保险箱的行为(或技术)
vocabulary.context: Out of pure curiosity, Feynman made himself an expert on cracking safes, not only looking at the mechanics of safecracking but also cobbling together psychological insights about people who used safes and set the locks.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 出于纯粹的好奇心，费曼让自己成为了破解保险箱的专家，他不仅研究了破解保险箱的机制，还拼凑了有关使用保险箱和开锁的人的心理洞察

vocabulary.definition: n. 时事通讯, 业务通讯, 时事传报；[法] 时事通讯, 时事信札
vocabulary.context: Later, he branched off into publishing newsletters for clients.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 后来，他开始为客户出版时事通讯

vocabulary.definition: n. staunch的变形
vocabulary.context: Resilient people and companies face reality with staunchness, make meaning of hardship instead of crying out in despair, and improvise solutions from thin air.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有韧性的人和企业会坚定地面对现实，理解困难而不是绝望地呼喊，凭空想出解决方案

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无效地; 不起作用地; 无能力地
vocabulary.context: Currer Bell, George Eliot, George Sand, all the victims of inner strife as their writings prove, sought ineffectively to veil themselves by using the name of a man.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 柯勒·贝尔、乔治·艾略特、乔治·桑，正如他们的著作所证明的那样，他们都是内心冲突的受害者，他们试图通过使用男人的名字来掩盖自己，但效果不佳

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不显眼的, 不引人注意的
vocabulary.context: ...seems a little queer as it is, unreal, lop-sided; but why should they not add a supplement to history, calling it, of course, by some inconspicuous name so that women might figure there without impropriety?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...看起来有点奇怪，不真实，不平衡；但为什么他们不应该为历史添加补充，当然，用一些不起眼的名字来称呼它，以便妇女可以在其中出现而不会出现不当行为？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无实体的, 灵魂的, 无形体的；[法] 无形体的, 非物质的, 精神的
vocabulary.context: But when the web is pulled askew, hooked up at the edge, torn in the middle, one remembers that these webs are not spun in mid-air by incorporeal creatures, but are the work of suffering human beings, and are attached to grossly material things, like health and money and the houses we live in.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，当网被拉斜、边缘钩住、中间撕裂时，人们会记得这些网不是由虚体生物在半空中纺成的，而是受苦的人类的作品，并且依附于粗俗的物质事物，例如健康、金钱和我们居住的房屋

vocabulary.definition: adv. 散文式地, 平凡地
vocabulary.context: What one must do to bring her to life was to think poetically and prosaically at one and the same moment, thus keeping in touch with fact - that she is Mrs Martin, aged thirty-six, dressed in blue, wearing a black hat and brown shoes; but not losing sight of fiction either - that...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 要使她栩栩如生，我们必须在同一时刻以诗意和平淡的方式思考，从而与事实保持联系--她是马丁夫人，三十六岁，穿着蓝色衣服，戴着黑色帽子和棕色鞋子；她是马丁夫人，三十六岁，穿着蓝色衣服，戴着黑色帽子和棕色鞋子；但也不要忽视小说--那......

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可憎的, 可恶的
vocabulary.context: Thus they did homage to the convention, which if not implanted by the other sex was liberally encouraged by them (the chief glory of a woman is not to be talked of, said Pericles, himself a much-talked-of man) that publicity in women is detestable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，他们确实向这一惯例致敬，即使不是由异性植入，他们也慷慨地鼓励这一惯例（伯里克利本人是一个备受谈论的男人，他说，女人的主要荣耀是不值得谈论的），即女性的出名是可憎的

vocabulary.definition: n. 厌恶女人的人
vocabulary.context: 'It remains a strange and almost inexplicable fact that in Athena's city, where women were kept in almost Oriental suppression as odalisques or drudges, the stage should yet have produced figures like Clytemnestra and Cassandra Atossa and Antigone, Phedre and Medea, and all the other heroines who dominate play after play of the "misogynist" Euripides.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "这仍然是一个奇怪且几乎无法解释的事实，在雅典娜的城市里，妇女被当作宫女或苦工，受到近乎东方的压制，舞台上却应该出现像克吕泰涅斯特拉、卡桑德拉·阿托莎、安提戈涅、费德雷和美狄亚这样的人物，以及所有其他在"厌恶女性主义者"欧里庇得斯的戏剧中占据主导地位的女英雄

vocabulary.definition: n. 自我心理分析
vocabulary.context: And one gathers from this enormous modern literature of confession and self-analysis that to write a work of genius is almost always a feat of prodigious difficulty.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人们从大量的现代忏悔和自我分析文献中了解到，写出一部天才的作品几乎总是一项极其困难的壮举

vocabulary.definition: adj. 顺利的, 适合的, 吉祥的, 慈悲的
vocabulary.context: But what is the state of mind that is most propitious to the act of creation?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是什么心态最有利于创造行为呢？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非物质的, 无形的, 精神的；[法] 非物质的, 无形的, 非实质的
vocabulary.context: Such material difficulties were formidable; but much worse were the immaterial.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种物质困难是巨大的；但更糟糕的是非物质的

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 订婚, 许配；[法] 订婚
vocabulary.context: Soon, however, before she was out of her teens, she was to be betrothed to the son of a neighbouring woolstapler.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，不久之后，在她还不到十几岁的时候，她就被许配给了邻近的羊毛纺织工的儿子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 合资的, 股份组织的
vocabulary.context: Society is a joint-stock company in which the members agree for the better securing of his bread to each shareholder, to surrender the liberty and culture of the eater, the virtue in most request is conformity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 协会是一家股份公司，其成员同意为了更好地确保每个股东的面包安全，放弃食者的自由和文化，大多数要求的美德是服从

vocabulary.definition: n. 包装用线, 缝线, 两股线
vocabulary.context: Sew them up with packthread, do.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 用包线把它们缝起来，做

vocabulary.definition: n. 怀抱大志者, 野心家；adj. 上进的, 有野心的
vocabulary.context: And we are now men, and must accept in the highest mind the same transcendent destiny; and not pinched in a corner, not cowards fleeing before a revolution, but redeemers and benefactors, pious aspirants to be noble clay plastic under the Almighty effort, let us advance and advance on Chaos and the Dark...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们现在是人，必须在最高的心灵中接受同样的超然命运；不是被困在角落里，不是在革命前逃跑的懦夫，而是救赎者和捐助者，虔诚的有志者在全能的努力下成为高贵的粘土塑料，让我们在混沌和黑暗中不断前进......

vocabulary.definition: adj. 卓越的, 出类拔萃的, 超常的；n. 卓越的人, 超绝物
vocabulary.context: And we are now men, and must accept in the highest mind the same transcendent destiny; and not pinched in a corner, not cowards fleeing before a revolution, but redeemers and benefactors, pious aspirants to be noble clay plastic under the Almighty effort, let us advance and advance on Chaos and the Dark...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们现在是人，必须在最高的心灵中接受同样的超然命运；不是被困在角落里，不是在革命前逃跑的懦夫，而是救赎者和捐助者，虔诚的有志者在全能的努力下成为高贵的粘土塑料，让我们在混沌和黑暗中不断前进......

vocabulary.definition: n. 买回者, 赎当者, 赎身者, 救世主；[法] 赎买者, 履行者, 偿还者
vocabulary.context: And we are now men, and must accept in the highest mind the same transcendent destiny; and not pinched in a corner, not cowards fleeing before a revolution, but redeemers and benefactors, pious aspirants to be noble clay plastic under the Almighty effort, let us advance and advance on Chaos and the Dark...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们现在是人，必须在最高的心灵中接受同样的超然命运；不是被困在角落里，不是在革命前逃跑的懦夫，而是救赎者和捐助者，虔诚的有志者在全能的努力下成为高贵的粘土塑料，让我们在混沌和黑暗中不断前进......

vocabulary.definition: n. 不相信英国国教的人, 不符合通常准则的人, 不落俗套的人；adj. 违反准则的, 不落俗套的, 违反规范的
vocabulary.context: Whoso would be a man must be a nonconformist.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个人必须是一个不墨守成规的人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 相称的, 成比例的, 适当的；v.t. 使相称, 使成比例
vocabulary.context: It may be safely trusted as proportionate and of good issues, so it be faithfully imparted, but God will not have his work made manifest by cowards.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它可以被安全地相信是适当的并且是好的问题，因此它可以被忠实地传达，但上帝不会让懦夫彰显他的工作

vocabulary.definition: n. 解脱, 解救, 释放, 判决；[法] 交保, 判决, 正式意见
vocabulary.context: It is a deliverance which does not deliver.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是一种不拯救的拯救

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 掌握, 控制, 支配；v.i. 占主导地位, 占优势
vocabulary.context: Great men have always done so and confided themselves childlike to the genius of their age, betraying their perception that the Eternal was stirring at their heart, working through their hands, predominating in all their being.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 伟人总是这样做，他们像孩子一样向他们那个时代的天才倾诉自己，背叛了他们的看法，即永恒在他们的心中激动，通过他们的双手工作，在他们的一切存在中占主导地位

vocabulary.definition: n. 深谋远虑, 天意, 天命
vocabulary.context: Accept the place the divine Providence has found for you; the society of your contemporaries, the connection of events.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 接受神圣的天意为你找到的地方；你同时代的社会，事件的联系

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不忠贞的, 背信的, 有异心的
vocabulary.context: Cosmus, Duke of Florence, had a desperate saying against perfidious or neglecting friends, as if those wrongs were unpardonable: You shall read (saith he) that we are commanded to forgive our enemies, but you never read that we are commanded to forgive our friends .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 佛罗伦萨公爵科斯穆斯对背信弃义或忽视的朋友说了一句绝望的话，仿佛这些错误是不可原谅的：你会读到（他说）我们被命令宽恕我们的敌人，但你从未读过我们被命令宽恕我们的朋友

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有报仇心的, 怀恨的, 惩罚的；[法] 报复的, 有报仇心的, 复仇的
vocabulary.context: Nay rather, vindictive persons live the life of witches; who as they are mischievous, so end they unfortunate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 更确切地说，有报复心的人过着女巫的生活。因为他们很顽皮，所以他们的结局很不幸

vocabulary.definition: n. [植]石南；荆棘；用石南根制成之烟斗
vocabulary.context: And if any man should do wrong merely out of ill nature, why, yet it is but like the thorn or briar, which prick and scratch, because they can do no other.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果一个人仅仅出于邪恶的本性而做错事，那它就像荆棘或荆棘一样，会刺痛和抓伤，因为他们别无选择

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不可原谅的, 不可宽恕的
vocabulary.context: Cosmus, Duke of Florence, had a desperate saying against perfidious or neglecting friends, as if those wrongs were unpardonable: You shall read (saith he) that we are commanded to forgive our enemies, but you never read that we are commanded to forgive our friends .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 佛罗伦萨公爵科斯穆斯对背信弃义或忽视的朋友说了一句绝望的话，仿佛这些错误是不可原谅的：你会读到（他说）我们被命令宽恕我们的敌人，但你从未读过我们被命令宽恕我们的朋友

vocabulary.definition: adj. ays的古体
vocabulary.context: And Salomon, I am sure, saith, It is the glory of a man to pass by an offence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我确信所罗门说过，越过过犯是一个人的荣耀

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可容忍的, 可以的
vocabulary.context: The most tolerable sort of revenge is for those wrongs which there is no law to remedy; but then let a man take heed the revenge be such as there is no law to punish; else a man's enemy is still beforehand, and it is two for one.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最可容忍的报复是针对那些没有法律可以补救的错误；但是，一个人要小心，报复的力度要达到没有法律惩罚的程度。否则人之敌还在前，那就是二打一了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 狡诈的, 诡计多端的, 巧妙的
vocabulary.context: For the delight seemeth to be not so much in doing the hurt as in making the party repent: but base and crafty cowards are like the arrow that flieth in the dark.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因为快乐似乎并不在于造成伤害，而在于让一方悔改：但卑鄙而狡猾的懦夫就像黑暗中飞行的箭

vocabulary.definition: n. 犯罪, 冒犯, 违反, 罪过, 过错, 攻击；[法] 犯法, 罪过, 过错
vocabulary.context: And Salomon, I am sure, saith, It is the glory of a man to pass by an offence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我确信所罗门说过，越过过犯是一个人的荣耀

vocabulary.definition: n. 内容, 满足, 意义, 要旨；adj. 满足的, 满意的；v.t. 使...满足, 使...安心；[计] 内容
vocabulary.context: But yet the spirit of Job was in a better tune: Shall we (saith he) take good at God's hands, and not be content to take evil also?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但约伯的精神却更好了：我们（他说）是否应该接受上帝手中的好处，而不是也接受邪恶？

vocabulary.definition: n. 悲剧演员, 悲剧作家
vocabulary.context: It was when the face and figure of a great tragedian began to haunt her imagination and stir her senses.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就在那时，一位伟大悲剧家的面孔和身材开始萦绕在她的想象中，搅动着她的感官

vocabulary.definition: n. 真诚, 真挚；真实, 真正
vocabulary.context: The persistence of the infatuation lent it an aspect of genuineness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 持续的痴情赋予它真诚的一面

vocabulary.definition: n. 敏捷, 机敏
vocabulary.context: But remembering Edna's whimsical turn of mind of late, and foreseeing that she had immediately acted upon her impetuous determination, he grasped the situation with his usual promptness and handled it with his well-known business tact and cleverness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但想起埃德娜最近反复无常的心思，并预见到她会立即按照自己的冲动行事，他以一贯的敏捷抓住了局势，并以他众所周知的商业机智和聪明才智处理了这件事

vocabulary.definition: n. 暗流, 潜流, 电流不足
vocabulary.context: She began to look with her own eyes; to see and to apprehend the deeper undercurrents of life.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她开始用自己的眼睛看；去观察并理解生活中更深层的潜流

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再评价, 再摊派, 再课税
vocabulary.context: Edna gradually reassesses her priorities and takes a more active role in her own happiness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 埃德娜逐渐重新评估她的优先事项，并在自己的幸福中发挥更积极的作用

vocabulary.definition: n. 作壁画的程序或方法, 壁画装饰
vocabulary.context: There was to be an addition-a small snuggery; there was to be frescoing, and hardwood flooring was to be put into such rooms as had not yet been subjected to this improvement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有一个补充--一个小舒适；尚未进行此项改进的房间将进行壁画和硬木地板铺设

vocabulary.definition: n. 家务, 家庭
vocabulary.context: It might get noised about that the Pontelliers had met with reverses, and were forced to conduct their menage on a humbler scale than heretofore.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 庞特利埃家族遭遇挫折，被迫以比以往更卑微的规模进行管理，这可能会引起人们的关注

vocabulary.definition: n. 温暖舒适的地方, 舒适的私室, 旅馆中的酒吧间
vocabulary.context: There was to be an addition-a small snuggery; there was to be frescoing, and hardwood flooring was to be put into such rooms as had not yet been subjected to this improvement.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 还有一个补充--一个小舒适；尚未进行此项改进的房间将进行壁画和硬木地板铺设

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使苦恼；v.i. 生病, 处境困难；[计] 数组互联逻辑
vocabulary.context: He had gone to bed perfectly well, she said, and nothing had ailed him all day.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她说，他睡得很好，一整天没有任何不适

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 惠赐, 允许, 给予；[法] 准予, 惠予, 俯允
vocabulary.context: This may seem like a ponderous weight of wisdom to descend upon the soul of a young woman of twenty-eight-perhaps more wisdom than the Holy Ghost is usually pleased to vouchsafe to any woman.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于一个二十八岁的年轻女子的灵魂来说，这似乎是一个沉重的智慧负担，也许比圣灵通常乐意赐予任何女人的智慧还要多

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 开除...教籍, 逐出教会；adj. 被逐出教会的；n. 被逐出教会的人
vocabulary.context: Why does it always crucify Christ, and excommunicate Copernicus and Luther, and pronounce Washington and Franklin rebels?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为什么它总是把基督钉在十字架上，将哥白尼和路德逐出教会，并宣布华盛顿和富兰克林为叛逆者？

vocabulary.definition: n. 镇民, 城里人, 市民, 同镇人, 同乡, 同市人
vocabulary.context: I have heard some of my townsmen say, "I should like to have them order me out to help put down an insurrection of the slaves, or to march to Mexico; - see if I would go"; and yet these very men have each, directly by their allegiance, and so indirectly, at least, by their money, furnished a substitute.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我听到我的一些同乡说："我想让他们命令我出去帮助镇压奴隶的叛乱，或者进军墨西哥； --看看我是否愿意去"；然而，正是这些人，通过他们的效忠直接地，并且间接地，至少通过他们的金钱，提供了一个替代品

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不属于道德范畴的, 无道德观念的；[法] 不道德的, 不发生道德问题的
vocabulary.context: After the first blush of sin comes its indifference; and from immoral it becomes, as it were, unmoral, and not quite unnecessary to that life which we have made.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第一次犯罪脸红之后，随之而来的是冷漠；可以说，它从不道德变成了不道德，而且对于我们所创造的生活来说并不是完全不必要的

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 违反, 犯罪, 侵犯；v.i. 越界, 违法
vocabulary.context: Unjust laws exist: shall we be content to obey them, or shall we endeavor to amend them, and obey them until we have succeeded, or shall we transgress them at once?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 不公正的法律是存在的：我们应该满足于遵守它们，还是应该努力修改它们，并遵守它们直到我们成功，或者我们应该立即违反它们？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 忏悔的, 悔悟的, 表示悔罪的；n. 忏悔的人, 悔罪者
vocabulary.context: ...which makes the war; is applauded by those whose own act and authority he disregards and sets at naught; as if the state were penitent to that degree that it differed one to scourge it while it sinned, but not to that degree that it left off sinning for a moment.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...导致战争；受到那些他无视和蔑视自己行为和权威的人的赞扬；就好像国家的忏悔程度达到了人们在它犯罪时对其进行鞭打的程度，但还没有达到它暂时停止犯罪的程度

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使震动, 使震撼, 使抽搐
vocabulary.context: So is all change for the better, like birth and death, which convulse the body.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所有向好的方向变化也是如此，就像出生和死亡一样，都会使身体抽搐

vocabulary.definition: n. 正式请求, 申请, 需要, 征用, 通知单, 申请书, 引渡要求；v.t. 征用, 要求
vocabulary.context: Some are petitioning the State to dissolve the Union, to disregard the requisitions of the President.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些人请求国家解散联邦，无视总统的要求

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无私欲的, 廉洁的, 公平的
vocabulary.context: The broadest and most prevalent error requires the most disinterested virtue to sustain it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最广泛和最普遍的错误需要最无私的美德来维持它

vocabulary.definition: n. 吝啬；卑鄙；劣等
vocabulary.context: Thus, under the name of Order and Civil Government, we are all made at last to pay homage to and support our own meanness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，在秩序和公民政府的名义下，我们最终都被迫向自己的卑鄙表示敬意和支持

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有效果的, 会应验的, 有法律效力的
vocabulary.context: If you are cheated out of a single dollar by your neighbor, you do not rest satisfied with knowing that you are cheated, or with saying that you are cheated, or even with petitioning him to pay you your due; but you take effectual steps at once to obtain the full amount, and see that you are never cheated again.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果你被邻居骗走了一美元，你不会满足于知道自己被骗了，或者说你被骗了，甚至请求他支付你应得的费用；但您立即采取有效措施获得全额金额，并确保您再也不会被骗

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不剃须的, 未修面的
vocabulary.context: A red-haired, unshaven, untidy man sat in a rocking chair by a window.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个红发、胡子拉碴、邋遢的男人坐在窗边的摇椅上

vocabulary.definition: n. （在对神默祷中的）自我消灭
vocabulary.context: And next came the primal instinct of self-preservation-or was it self-annihilation, the instinct that society has grafted on the natural branch?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 接下来是自我保护的原始本能--或者是自我毁灭，社会嫁接在自然枝条上的本能？

vocabulary.definition: n. 氯仿；v.t. 用氯仿麻醉
vocabulary.context: If it wasn't for peroxide the chloroform limit would have put her out long ago.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果不是过氧化氢，氯仿限值早就让她出局了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 穿便衣的；[法] 便衣, 便服
vocabulary.context: Up stepped a plain-clothes man-there were lots of 'em there, dressed exactly like preachers and hustling us spirits around just like cops do on earth-and by the arm he dragged-whom, do you think?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个便衣男子走上前来--那里有很多人，穿着一模一样的传教士，像地球上的警察一样催促着我们的灵魂--他拖着一只手臂--你认为是谁？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 光着头的, 不戴帽的；adv. 光着头, 不戴帽
vocabulary.context: "That was two years ago, when she used to came down to the corner of Chrystie bare-headed to meet you after supper.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "那是两年前的事了，当时她常常在晚饭后光着头来到克里斯蒂的街角与你见面

vocabulary.definition: n. 烈性子的人, 喷火的东西
vocabulary.context: "She's a little spitfire, sometimes, now," said the Kid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "她现在有时有点脾气暴躁，"孩子说

vocabulary.definition: n. 丘鹬
vocabulary.context: Liz ran out and down the street swift and true as a woodcock flying through a grove of saplings at dusk.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莉兹跑出去，沿着街道迅速而真实地跑去，就像黄昏时分飞过树苗林的山鹬一样

vocabulary.definition: adj. 双月的
vocabulary.context: It was the bi-monthly dance, a dress affair in which the members took great pride and bestirred themselves huskily to further and adorn.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是每两个月一次的舞会，是一场盛装盛会，成员们对此感到非常自豪，并沙哑地鼓起勇气进一步装饰

vocabulary.definition: adj. 黄昏的, 薄暮的, (花)晚上开的
vocabulary.context: Odors from strong bacon and boiling coffee contended against the cut-plug fumes from the vespertine pipe.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 浓烈的培根和煮咖啡的气味与 vespertine 烟斗中的割塞烟雾相互竞争

vocabulary.definition: n. 打赌者；[法] 赌徒, 打赌者
vocabulary.context: "And, oh, what'll be done to you'll be a plenty," said a bettor, with anticipatory glee.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "而且，哦，你会得到很多，"一位投注者带着期待的喜悦说道

vocabulary.definition: n. 军事训练, 军人, 军队
vocabulary.context: patience or lenience Senate by Fabius Maximus, and called the corrupter of the Roman soldiery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 法比乌斯·马克西姆斯提出的耐心或宽大元老院，并被称为罗马士兵的腐败者

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 当做遗产让与；n. 罗马教皇使节, 特使, 使节, 使者
vocabulary.context: The Locrians were laid waste by a legate of Scipio, yet they were not avenged by him, nor was the insolence of the legate punished, owing entirely to his easy nature.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 洛克里亚人被西庇阿的使节蹂躏，但他没有为他们报仇，使节的傲慢也没有受到惩罚，这完全是由于他的随和性格

vocabulary.definition: n. 仁慈；宽厚
vocabulary.context: patience or lenience Senate by Fabius Maximus, and called the corrupter of the Roman soldiery.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 法比乌斯·马克西姆斯提出的耐心或宽大元老院，并被称为罗马士兵的腐败者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 可改变的
vocabulary.context: ...his soldiers more license than is consistent with military discipline. For this he was upbraided in the 3. Fickle(adjective)easily changeable; indecisive 4. Covetous(adjective)jealous 5. Abstain(verb)to leave alone 6. inherited land; fatherland 7. listed or mentionned 8. argument or grievance 9. patience or lenience Senate by Fabius Maximus,...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...他的士兵比军事纪律更自由。为此他在 3. Fickle（形容词）容易改变；优柔寡断 4. 贪婪（形容词）嫉妒 5. 弃权（动词）不去管 6. 继承的土地；祖国 7. 列出或提及 8. 争论或不满 9. 耐心或宽大 参议院法比乌斯·马克西姆斯 (Fabius Maximus)，...

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有害的, 不公正的；[法] 伤害的, 有害的, 不公平的
vocabulary.context: This disposition, if he had been continued in the command, would have destroyed in time the fame and glory of Scipio; but, he being under the control of the Senate, this injurious characteristic not only concealed itself, but contributed to his glory.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种性格，如果他继续指挥的话，迟早会毁掉西庇阿的名声和荣耀。但是，由于他处于元老院的控制之下，这种有害的特征不仅被掩盖了，而且还助长了他的荣耀

vocabulary.definition: adv. 到这样的程度, 如此地, 因为, 由于
vocabulary.context: Insomuch that someone in the Senate, wishing to excuse him, said there were many men who knew much better how not to err than to correct the errors of others.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 以至于参议院有人想为他辩解，他说有很多人比纠正别人的错误更懂得如何不犯错误

vocabulary.definition: adj. (英)近视的, 眼光短浅的, 目光短浅的；[医] 近视的
vocabulary.context: And short-sighted writers admire his deeds from one point of view and from another condemn the principal cause of them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 而目光短浅的作家从一个角度钦佩他的行为，从另一个角度谴责他的行为的主要原因

vocabulary.definition: n. 祖传的财物, 继承物；[经] 世袭财产, 遗产
vocabulary.context: But when it is necessary for him to proceed against the life of someone, he must do it on proper justification and for manifest cause, but above all things he must keep his hands off the property of others, because men more quickly forget the death of their father than the loss of their patrimony.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，当他有必要对某人的生命采取行动时，他必须以适当的理由和明显的理由这样做，但最重要的是，他必须把手放在他人的财产上，因为人们比忘记失去遗产更快地忘记父亲的死亡

vocabulary.definition: n. 节制, 自制, 忍耐；[法] 债务偿还期的延展, 忍耐, 宽容
vocabulary.context: ...times but within the memory of man, against whom, nevertheless, his army rebelled in Spain; this arose from nothing but his too great forbearance, which gave his soldiers more license than is consistent with military discipline.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...多次，但在人类的记忆中，尽管如此，他的军队在西班牙反抗了他们；这完全是因为他过于克制，这给了他的士兵更多的自由，超出了军事纪律的范围

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 分发, 分配；v.i. 特许, 豁免
vocabulary.context: During the rioting between the Cancellieri and Panciatichi factions in 1502 and 1503 dispensed with.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1502年和1503年Cancellieri派和Panciatichi派之间的骚乱期间被免除

vocabulary.definition: adj. 往西南的, 来自西南的；adv. 往西南, 来自西南
vocabulary.context: I knew that I must travel in a southwesterly direction to reach my destination, but the sun was my only guide.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我知道我必须向西南方向行驶才能到达目的地，但太阳是我唯一的向导

vocabulary.definition: adv. 鲁莽地, 不小心地
vocabulary.context: I had certainly acted imprudently.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我的行为确实很不谨慎

vocabulary.definition: adj. 流血的, 血腥的, 嗜血的
vocabulary.context: Thanks to the lessons of Felix and the sanguinary laws of man, I had learned now to work mischief.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 感谢菲利克斯的教训和人类的血腥法则，我现在学会了恶作剧

vocabulary.definition: n. 芳香, 爽快
vocabulary.context: One morning, however, finding that my path lay through a deep wood, I ventured to continue my journey after the sun had risen; the day, which was one of the first of spring, cheered even me by the loveliness of its sunshine and the balminess of the air.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，有一天早上，我发现我的路穿过一片茂密的树林，于是我冒险在太阳升起后继续我的旅程。这一天是春天的第一天，美丽的阳光和宜人的空气甚至让我感到高兴

vocabulary.definition: n. 善良, 温厚
vocabulary.context: ...creatures could bestow and that she whose resemblance I contemplated would, in regarding me, have changed that air of divine benignity to one expressive of disgust and affright.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ......生物可以赐予我，而我所想到的她的相似之处，在看待我时，会把那种神圣仁慈的神情变成一种厌恶和恐惧的表情

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 祈求, 诅咒
vocabulary.context: How often did I imprecate curses on the cause of my being!
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我有多少次咒骂我的存在！

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无热的

And like beasts they blindly gave birth to children! Is it not strange to understand gardening, chicken farming, fishery (we have definite knowledge that they were familiar with all these...

vocabulary.context: For the doctors could not force him to eat, either, and though he was fed intravenously, it was clear from the beginning that there was no hope for him.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因为医生也不能强迫他进食，虽然他是通过静脉注射喂养的，但从一开始就很明显他没有希望

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不能想像地；难以理解地
vocabulary.context: He had always been so strange and had lived, like a prophet, in such unimaginably close communion with the Lord that his long silences which were punctuated by moans and hallelujahs and snatches of old songs while he sat at the living-room window never seemed odd to us.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他总是那么奇怪，就像一位先知一样，与主有着难以想象的密切交流，以至于当他坐在客厅窗前时，他长时间的沉默，不时地发出呻吟、哈利路亚和老歌的片段，对我们来说从来没有觉得奇怪

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不和谐的, 刺耳的, 不一致的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我看来，上帝亲自设计了最持久、最残酷的不和谐的尾声，以纪念我父亲的终结

vocabulary.definition: n. 下等酒馆( honky-tonk的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: He had been born in New Orleans and had been a quite young man there during the time that Louis Armstrong, a boy, was running errands for the dives and honky-tonks of what was always presented to me as one of the most wicked of cities - to this day, whenever I think of New Orleans, I also helplessly think of Sodom and Gomorrah.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他出生在新奥尔良，在路易斯·阿姆斯特朗（Louis Armstrong）还是个男孩的时候，他就在那里为一个在我看来是最邪恶的城市之一的潜水店和酒吧跑腿的时候，一直是个相当年轻的人--直到今天，每当我想到新奥尔良，我也会无助地想到所多玛和蛾摩拉

vocabulary.definition: n. 尾音, 乐章结尾部, 结尾段

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在我看来，上帝亲自设计了最持久、最残酷的不和谐的尾声，以纪念我父亲的终结

vocabulary.definition: n. 不妥协, 强硬, 不让步
vocabulary.context: He had been ill a long time - in the mind, as we now realized, reliving instances of his fantastic intransigence in the new light of his affliction and endeavoring to feel a sorrow for him which never, quite, came true.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他已经病了很长一段时间了--正如我们现在意识到的那样，在他的心灵中，在他的痛苦的新光中重温他奇妙的不妥协的实例，并努力为他感到悲伤，但这从未真正成为现实

vocabulary.definition: adv. 不可名状地
vocabulary.context: He could be chilling in the pulpit and indescribably cruel in his personal life and he was certainly the most bitter man I have ever met; yet it must be said that there was something else in him, buried in him, which lent him his tremendous power and, even, a rather crushing charm.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他在讲坛上可能令人不寒而栗，在个人生活中则难以形容的残酷，他无疑是我见过的最痛苦的人；但必须指出的是，他身上还埋藏着某种别的东西，这赋予了他巨大的力量，甚至一种相当具有压倒性的魅力

vocabulary.definition: v. 尝试, 试图( endeavor的现在分词 )
vocabulary.context: He had been ill a long time - in the mind, as we now realized, reliving instances of his fantastic intransigence in the new light of his affliction and endeavoring to feel a sorrow for him which never, quite, came true.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他已经病了很长一段时间了--正如我们现在意识到的那样，在他的心灵中，在他的痛苦的新光中重温他奇妙的不妥协的实例，并努力为他感到悲伤，但这从未真正成为现实

vocabulary.context: ...always became fretful and began to cry; when he tried to help one of us with our homework the absolutely unabating tension which emanated from him caused our minds and our tongues to become paralyzed, so that he, scarcely knowing why, flew into a rage and the child, not knowing why, was punished.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...总是变得焦躁并开始哭泣；当他试图帮助我们中的一个人做作业时，他身上散发出的绝对不减的紧张感使我们的思想和舌头变得麻痹，以至于他几乎不知道为什么会勃然大怒，而孩子也不知道为什么而受到了惩罚

vocabulary.context: It can also help you re-evaluate "filler" friendships: relationships you maintain because you'd rather do anything on a Friday night besides staying at home by yourself, even at the cost of spending time with people whose company you don't enjoy.

vocabulary.definition: v. (使)减压
vocabulary.context: The freedom of not having to follow the lead of others, with "no pressure to do anything, no pressure to talk to anyone, no obligation to make plans with people," is a great way to process and decompress, even for highly social individuals, Ms.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 不必跟随他人的自由，"没有做任何事情的压力，没有与任何人交谈的压力，没有义务与人制定计划"，是一种很好的处理和减压方式，即使对于高度社交的人来说也是如此

vocabulary.definition: adj. 自觉的, 怕难为情的
vocabulary.context: It also helps us discover new interests and ideas without having to worry about the opinions of others - one study even showed that teens are less self-conscious when they're alone.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它还可以帮助我们发现新的兴趣和想法，而不必担心他人的意见 - 一项研究甚至表明，青少年在独处时不那么难为情

vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...身体；给予她最大的思想和行动自由；彻底摆脱一切形式的束缚、习俗、依赖、迷信；摆脱恐惧的所有严重影响 - 是她个人生活的孤独和个人责任

vocabulary.definition: n. 职业, 表白, 声明；[化] 工种; 职业

vocabulary.definition: n. 子孙, 后裔, 成果；[医] 后代, 后裔
vocabulary.context: "Books are not absolutely dead things, but do contain a potency of life in them as to be as active as that soul was whose progeny they are," wrote John Milton, author of Paradise Lost , in his 1644 book Areopagitica .
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 《失乐园》的作者约翰·弥尔顿在他 1644 年出版的《Areopagitica》一书中写道："书籍并不是绝对死的东西，但它们确实蕴含着一种生命的力量，就像它们的后代的灵魂一样活跃。"

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在书籍受到潜在纵火犯威胁的漫长历史中，这是一次非凡的胜利，而且是相对罕见的胜利
