vocabulary.contextTranslation: 席卷欧洲和美国的痛苦将阻碍公正和平的实现，并危及下一代

vocabulary.definition: n. 互殴
vocabulary.context: She told this story each time my real sister, Alexandra, and I were standing, arms crossed, facing away from each other after some squabble or fistfight.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每当我和我真正的妹妹亚历山德拉（Alexandra）在争吵或打架后站着，双臂交叉，背对着对方时，她都会讲这个故事

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 补助, 资助, 贿赂；[经] 资助, 津贴, 给...补助金
vocabulary.context: The government subsidizes public housing, meaning that the government pays for part of the housing in order to keep rent costs low.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 政府补贴公共住房，意味着政府支付部分住房费用，以保持较低的租金成本

vocabulary.definition: n. 本地话, 方言；adj. 地方的, 用本地语写成的
vocabulary.context: I know its grammar, its parts of speech; I know the subtleties of 2 3 its idioms, its vernacular words and phrases to which the native speaker has never given a 1.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我知道它的语法，它的词类；我知道 2 3 它的习语、白话单词和短语的微妙之处，而母语人士从未给过 1 分

vocabulary.definition: n. 习语, 成语, 惯用语法, 方言
vocabulary.context: Idiom (noun)a common expression in a language, often easily understood by native speakers but memorized and learned by outsiders.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 习语（名词）一种语言中的常见表达方式，通常很容易被母语者理解，但被外地人记住和学习

vocabulary.definition: n. （居民拥挤, 住屋破旧的）市中心平民区；adj. 市中心的
vocabulary.context: I am middle class, despite the fact that my parents had no money; I am white, but I grew up in an inner-city housing project where most everyone was black or Hispanic.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我是中产阶级，尽管我的父母没有钱；我是白人，但我在市中心的一个住房项目中长大，那里大多数人都是黑人或西班牙裔

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 争吵, 口角；n. 争吵, 口角
vocabulary.context: She told this story each time my real sister, Alexandra, and I were standing, arms crossed, facing away from each other after some squabble or fistfight.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每当我和我真正的妹妹亚历山德拉（Alexandra）在争吵或打架后站着，双臂交叉，背对着对方时，她都会讲这个故事

vocabulary.definition: adj. 先天的, 天生的；[医] 先天的, 生来的
vocabulary.context: I was fascinated that I could have been oblivious to something that years later feels so natural, so innate as race does.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 令我着迷的是，我本可以忽视一些多年后感觉如此自然、如此与生俱来的东西，就像种族一样

vocabulary.definition: adj. 遗忘的, 健忘的, 不知不觉的；[法] 忘却的, 健忘的, 不在意的
vocabulary.context: I was fascinated that I could have been oblivious to something that years later feels so natural, so innate as race does.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 令我着迷的是，我本可以忽视一些多年后感觉如此自然、如此与生俱来的东西，就像种族一样

vocabulary.definition: adj. 所谓的, 号称的；[法] 所谓的, 据称的
vocabulary.context: In fact, my childhood was like a social science experiment: Find out what being middle class really means by raising a kid from a so-called good family in a so-called "Lower East Side Manhattan" by Enrique bad neighborhood.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，我的童年就像一个社会科学实验：通过在恩里克所谓的"曼哈顿下东区"不良社区中抚养一个来自所谓好家庭的孩子，找出中产阶级的真正含义

vocabulary.definition: n. 露兜树； （用于编织的）露兜叶纤维
vocabulary.context: and drying pandanus leaves
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 和干燥露兜叶

vocabulary.definition: adj. 整齐的, 整洁的
vocabulary.context: We are skies uncluttered
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们的天空整洁

vocabulary.definition: n. 阿姨, 姨妈, 舅妈, 姑妈, 伯母
vocabulary.context: of grandmothers mothers aunties sisters -
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 祖母的母亲阿姨姐妹们-

vocabulary.definition: adj. 粗糙的, 粗硬的, 起老茧的；v. （使）硬结, （使）起茧( callous的过去式和过去分词 ); （使）冷酷无情
vocabulary.context: by calloused fingers
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 长满老茧的手指

vocabulary.definition: n. 嘎嚓声；v.i. 发出嘎嚓声, 嘎嚓响着前进
vocabulary.context: across a road clogged with chugging cars
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 穿过一条被嘎嘎作响的汽车堵塞的马路

vocabulary.definition: n. 航海家；[法] 领航员, 驾驶员, 航海者
vocabulary.context: of the finest navigators
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最优秀的航海家

vocabulary.definition: n. 泡沫聚苯乙烯
vocabulary.context: Tell them we are styrofoam cups of koolaid red
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 告诉他们我们是科莱德红的聚苯乙烯泡沫塑料杯

vocabulary.definition: adj. 宏伟的, 庄严的
vocabulary.context: majestic in their sweeping landscape
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 壮丽的风景

vocabulary.definition: n. 政治, 政治学, 政见, 政治活动；[法] 政治, 政治学, 政纲
vocabulary.context: of the politics
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 政治

vocabulary.definition: n. 外向性格的人；[医] 外倾[性格]者
vocabulary.context: We even controlled for personality traits, such as being an extrovert and being open to new experiences.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们甚至控制了性格特征，例如外向和乐于接受新体验

vocabulary.definition: adj. 〈心理〉前摄的
vocabulary.context: It could be a signal of proactive and creative choice for students who don't fit the mold in terms of how higher education is currently delivered.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于那些不符合目前高等教育模式的学生来说，这可能是一个主动和创造性选择的信号

vocabulary.definition: n. （大学）校园（campus的复数形式）
vocabulary.context: This could be done through existing courses or perhaps new centers and spaces dedicated to innovation on college campuses.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这可以通过现有课程或大学校园内致力于创新的新中心和空间来完成

vocabulary.definition: adj. 革新的, 创新的, 富有革新精神的
vocabulary.context: New research we conducted recently shows that double majors fare better in another way as well: They are more innovative.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们最近进行的新研究表明，双专业在另一个方面也表现得更好：它们更具创新性

vocabulary.definition: n. 思想者, 思想家
vocabulary.context: What employers really want - according to a recent survey - are graduates who can effectively work in diverse teams, are creative thinkers and have persuasive communication skills.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 根据最近的一项调查，雇主真正想要的是能够在多元化团队中有效工作、具有创造性思想家并具有有说服力的沟通技巧的毕业生

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有说服力的, 劝诱的, 劝导性的
vocabulary.context: These capacities include skills related to networking, persuasive communication, working on diverse teams, and risk taking.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些能力包括与网络、说服性沟通、在不同团队中工作以及承担风险相关的技能

vocabulary.definition: n. 路径, 途径；[医] 路径, 道
vocabulary.context: While certainly our data demonstrate that double-majors are the most innovative, we do not conclude that this academic pathway is always the best choice for students or industries.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然我们的数据确实表明双学位最具创新性，但我们并不认为这种学术途径始终是学生或行业的最佳选择

vocabulary.definition: n. 才能, 能力, 全体教员, (大学的)系；[医] 能力, [大学]院系
vocabulary.context: We also considered the institution students attended, the quality of teaching to which they were exposed and the nature of their interactions with faculty members.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们还考虑了学生就读的院校、他们所接受的教学质量以及他们与教职员工互动的性质

vocabulary.definition: adv. 相对地, 比较地, 相当地, 相关地；[计] 相对地
vocabulary.context: We did so using a relatively new survey instrument that enabled us to determine how institutions can help students develop their innovation capacities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们使用了一种相对较新的调查工具来做到这一点，该工具使我们能够确定机构如何帮助学生发展他们的创新能力

vocabulary.definition: n. 减除, 扣除, 减除额, 推论；[医] 演绎, 推论
vocabulary.context: In addition, recent tax changes that limit federal deductions for state taxes will increase pressure to keep state income and property tax rates down, further hindering state funding.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，最近的税收变化限制了联邦对州税的扣除，这将增加压低州收入和财产税率的压力，进一步阻碍州的资金投入

vocabulary.definition: n. 慈善, 仁慈, 博爱, 慈善事业, 慈善机构(团体)
vocabulary.context: The major source of this support is philanthropy, made up of earnings on endowments and annual gifts.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种支持的主要来源是慈善事业，由捐赠收入和年度礼物组成

vocabulary.definition: adj. 资金不足的
vocabulary.context: However, given the pressure on budgets resulting from underfunded pensions, Medicaid and K-12, I am not optimistic that students can count on increased support from states.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，考虑到养老金、医疗补助和 K-12 资金不足给预算带来的压力，我对学生能否指望各州增加支持并不乐观

vocabulary.definition: n. 捐助, 天赋, 才能；[经] 捐赠, 捐款, 养老金
vocabulary.context: Although it is true that the new "endowment tax" on large endowments and any changes to the tax deduction for charitable giving reduce the funds available for financial aid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管针对大额捐赠的新"捐赠税"以及慈善捐赠税收减免的任何变化确实减少了可用于经济援助的资金

vocabulary.definition: n. 不明确, 含糊；[计] 二义性; 多义性
vocabulary.context: We must do our best to educate students in a global environment, keeping pace with technological innovations, teaching critical thinking, fostering comfort with ambiguity, and graduating nimble leaders who will thrive in a rapidly changing era.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们必须尽最大努力在全球环境中教育学生，跟上技术创新的步伐，教授批判性思维，培养对模糊性的适应能力，并培养出在快速变化的时代蓬勃发展的灵活领导者

vocabulary.definition: adj. 制度的, 公共机构的, 学会的；[法] 组织机构的, 制度的, 公共机构的
vocabulary.context: Private nonprofit colleges and universities educate about 20 percent of all undergraduates. The "sticker price" at these institutions gives the impression that they are not accessible to low- and middle-income students. However, privates provide significant institutional aid.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 私立非营利学院和大学培养了约 20% 的本科生。这些机构的"标价"给人的印象是低收入和中等收入的学生无法进入。然而，私人提供了大量的机构援助

vocabulary.definition: n. 学费, 讲授
vocabulary.context: For instance, states now rely more heavily on tuition to finance their public colleges and universities than on government funding.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，各州现在更多地依赖学费来为其公立学院和大学提供资金，而不是政府资助

vocabulary.definition: adv. 节约地, 不浪费地, 节省地, 节俭地, 在经济上, 在经济学上
vocabulary.context: Private colleges and universities are also struggling to make ends meet, steering a record amount of tuition revenue toward grant aid for economically needy students.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 私立学院和大学也在努力维持收支平衡，将创纪录的学费收入用于为经济困难的学生提供资助

vocabulary.definition: adj. 机会主义的, 投机取巧的
vocabulary.context: Looting broke out as opportunistic thieves cleaned out abandoned stores for a second night.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 投机取巧的小偷连续第二天晚上清理废弃的商店，抢劫事件随之爆发

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不能动手术的；[医] 不能手术的, 不宜手术的
vocabulary.context: With bridges washed out, highways converted into canals, and power and communications lines inoperable, government officials ordered everyone still remaining out of the city.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于桥梁被冲毁，高速公路变成了运河，电力和通讯线路无法运行，政府官员命令所有仍然留在城外的人

vocabulary.definition: adj. 水上的,越过水域的
vocabulary.context: Parts of the 24-mile-long Lake Pontchartrain Causeway, the world's longest overwater highway bridge, were missing as well.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 24英里长的庞恰特雷恩湖堤道（世界上最长的水上公路桥）的部分区域也失踪了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不能防守的, 不能维持的, 支持不住的, (主张等)站不住脚的
vocabulary.context: "The magnitude of the situation is untenable," said Gov.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 州长说："情况的严重程度是站不住脚的。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 居住船, 游艇
vocabulary.context: Seen from the vantage point of a Jefferson Parish sheriff's helicopter transporting FEMA officials, vast stretches of the city resembled a community of houseboats.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从杰斐逊教区治安官乘坐的运送联邦应急管理局官员的直升机的有利位置看去，这座城市的大片地区就像一个船屋社区

vocabulary.definition: n. 打桩, 桩结构, 打桩工程；[机] 打椿
vocabulary.context: Louis Bay, all that remained of the entire community of 200 homes was pilings.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 圣路易斯湾，整个社区的 200 户住宅只剩下木桩

vocabulary.definition: adj. 整月的
vocabulary.context: As the sweep of the devastation became clear, President Bush cut short his monthlong summer vacation on Tuesday and returned to Washington, where he will meet on Wednesday with a task force established to coordinate the efforts of 14 federal agencies that will be involved in responding to the disaster.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随着灾难的严重程度逐渐明朗，布什总统周二缩短了为期一个月的暑假并返回华盛顿，周三他将与一个特别工作组会面，该特别工作组旨在协调参与应对灾难的 14 个联邦机构的工作

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 重新聚集；v.t. vi. 重新组合；v.i. 变更部署
vocabulary.context: The city government regrouped in Baton Rouge, 80 miles to the northwest.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 市政府在西北 80 英里的巴吞鲁日重新集结

vocabulary.definition: adj. 线向不正的,方向偏离的,不重合的,未对准的
vocabulary.context: Sections of Interstate 10, the principal artery through the city, had pieces missing or misaligned, as if the highway were an unfinished jigsaw puzzle.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 穿过城市的主要干道 10 号州际公路的部分路段存在缺失或错位的情况，就好像高速公路是一个未完成的拼图游戏

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 说错, 作虚伪叙述
vocabulary.context: Correction: September 2, 2005, Friday Because of an editing error, a front-page article on Wednesday about the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina misstated the day that President Bush returned to Washington from his vacation to meet with a task force coordinating disaster response.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 更正：2005年9月2日，星期五 由于编辑错误，周三一篇关于卡特里娜飓风后果的头版文章误述了布什总统休假返回华盛顿与协调救灾工作组会面的日期

vocabulary.definition: adj. (=cost-effective)有成本效益的,值得花钱的,合算的,划算的
vocabulary.context: Every student should have access to financial literacy advisers and tools that help students take the most cost-efficient way to achieve a degree.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每个学生都应该有机会获得金融知识顾问和工具，帮助学生采用最具成本效益的方式获得学位

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无可非议地
vocabulary.context: Universities like Penn State are justifiably proud of their high graduation rates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 像宾夕法尼亚州立大学这样的大学有理由为其高毕业率感到自豪

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 使固定, 使不变, 注视；v.t. 使固定, 注视
vocabulary.context: Interestingly, the conversation on access and affordability seems to be fixated on controlling, first and foremost, the increase in tuition.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有趣的是，关于入学机会和负担能力的讨论似乎首先集中于控制学费的增加

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有利的, 有益的
vocabulary.context: Sadly, they also don't have time to participate in advantageous activities, such as research or internships.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 可悲的是，他们也没有时间参加有利的活动，例如研究或实习

vocabulary.definition: adv. 非常地, 很, 颇
vocabulary.context: We need to broaden the framing of this discussion considerably.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们需要大大拓宽这一讨论的框架

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 变宽, 扩大；v.t. 放宽, 使扩大
vocabulary.context: We need to broaden the framing of this discussion considerably.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们需要大大拓宽这一讨论的框架

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 大量存在, 富于, 充满
vocabulary.context: Editor's note: When it comes to the cost of higher education in the U.S., signs of trouble abound.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 编者注：当谈到美国的高等教育成本时，麻烦的迹象比比皆是

vocabulary.definition: n. 基础结构, 基础设施；[经] 基础设施
vocabulary.context: On top of shrinking state appropriations there are more technology-intensive degrees in every field; an aging campus infrastructure; a sharp increase in compliance and regulations reporting; and soaring health care costs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 除了国家拨款缩减之外，各个领域的技术密集型学位也有所增加；校园基础设施老化；合规和法规报告急剧增加；以及不断飙升的医疗费用

vocabulary.definition: adj. 批评的, 决定性的, 危险的, 临界的；[医] 危象的; 临界的, 极期的
vocabulary.context: The simple fact is that timely completion of a degree is a critical mechanism to control total cost.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一个简单的事实是，及时完成学位是控制总成本的关键机制

vocabulary.definition: adj. 以盈利为目的的
vocabulary.context: Even though for-profit colleges get a bad rap for being predatory and leaving students saddled with debt but no degree, a significant number of private nonprofit and public colleges have the same issues.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管营利性大学因其掠夺性和让学生背负债务而没有学位而受到诟病，但大量私立非营利性和公立大学也存在同样的问题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 非赢利的；不以赢利为目的的
vocabulary.context: For example, many private nonprofits with small endowments depend heavily on tuition.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，许多捐赠金额较小的私人非营利组织严重依赖学费

vocabulary.definition: n. 国书, 凭据, 证明书；[经] 凭证, 证书
vocabulary.context: For many students, failing to complete college in six years means not only carrying debt but carrying debt without receiving a degree or credential, which means decreased job prospects.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于许多学生来说，六年内未能完成大学学业不仅意味着背负债务，而且还意味着在没有获得学位或证书的情况下背负债务，这意味着就业前景下降

vocabulary.definition: adj. 相当大的, 大小相当的
vocabulary.context: But depending on annual tuition and limited forms of other aid, colleges can hamstring students with sizable debt after graduation that then has a drag effect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但根据每年的学费和有限形式的其他援助，大学可能会在毕业后让学生背负巨额债务，从而产生拖累效应

vocabulary.definition: n. 后腿腱；v.t. 切断腿腱使成跛腿, 使瘫痪
vocabulary.context: But depending on annual tuition and limited forms of other aid, colleges can hamstring students with sizable debt after graduation that then has a drag effect.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但根据每年的学费和有限形式的其他援助，大学可能会在毕业后让学生背负巨额债务，从而产生拖累效应

vocabulary.definition: adj. 治疗的, 补救的, 矫正的；[医] 治疗的
vocabulary.context: These colleges aggressively seek to bring more students through the door who can pay tuition, whether out of pocket or through loans, even though many of these students are not "college ready," and need to take remedial courses and struggle academically.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些大学积极寻求招收更多有能力支付学费的学生，无论是自掏腰包还是通过贷款，尽管这些学生中的许多人还没有"做好上大学的准备"，需要参加补习课程并在学业上遇到困难

vocabulary.definition: adj. 预期的, 将来的；[经] 预期的, 未来的
vocabulary.context: So, how can prospective students identify problematic colleges that will likely saddle them with debt and where few of their fellow students will graduate?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那么，未来的学生如何识别那些可能让他们背负债务、并且很少有同学能够毕业的有问题的大学呢？

vocabulary.definition: adj. 成问题的, 未定的, 疑难的, 有疑问的, 盖然性的, 或然性的
vocabulary.context: So, how can prospective students identify problematic colleges that will likely saddle them with debt and where few of their fellow students will graduate?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那么，未来的学生如何识别那些可能让他们背负债务、并且很少有同学能够毕业的有问题的大学呢？

vocabulary.definition: n. 景色, 展望；v.t. 勘探, 勘察；v.i. 勘探, 有前途
vocabulary.context: For many students, failing to complete college in six years means not only carrying debt but carrying debt without receiving a degree or credential, which means decreased job prospects.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于许多学生来说，六年内未能完成大学学业不仅意味着背负债务，而且还意味着在没有获得学位或证书的情况下背负债务，这意味着就业前景下降

vocabulary.definition: n. 旗舰
vocabulary.context: Specifically, 51 percent of the surveyed women's college graduates earned advanced degrees, compared to 33 percent of liberal arts and 27 percent of flagship public university graduates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 具体而言，接受调查的女子大学毕业生中有 51% 获得了高级学位，而文科毕业生的这一比例为 33%，旗舰公立大学毕业生的这一比例为 27%

vocabulary.definition: adj. （在社会、机构或某活动领域等）男人主宰的，男权占主导地位的
vocabulary.context: The impact is especially notable when one looks at achievement in traditionally male-dominated fields, including science, technology, engineering and math (STEM).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当人们审视传统上由男性主导的领域（包括科学、技术、工程和数学（STEM））的成就时，这种影响尤其显着

vocabulary.definition: n. 联系, 从属关系；[医] 父权(判定私生儿的父亲)
vocabulary.context: ...for example, created its list of the 25 schools most responsible for advances in science - a list that tracks the undergraduate affiliations of winners of the highest recognition in the sciences - Bryn Mawr College made the list, even though that list did not account for women's historical and present-day barriers to success in STEM fields.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...例如，创建了对科学进步最负有责任的 25 所学校的名单 - 该名单追踪了科学领域最高认可的获奖者的本科背景 - 布林莫尔学院 (Bryn Mawr College) 榜上有名，尽管该名单没有考虑到女性在 STEM 领域取得成功的历史和当今障碍

vocabulary.definition: n. 石英；[化] 石英
vocabulary.context: When Quartz magazine, for example, created its list of the 25 schools most responsible for advances in science - a list that tracks the undergraduate affiliations of winners of the highest recognition in the...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，《Quartz》杂志列出了对科学进步最有贡献的 25 所学校的名单，该名单追踪了科学界最高认可的获奖者的本科背景......

vocabulary.definition: n. 公平, 公正；[经] 权益, 产权
vocabulary.context: Women's knowledge and skill is not all that's required for gender equity in the workplace, but it is a necessary condition.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 女性的知识和技能并不是工作场所性别平等所需要的全部，但它是一个必要条件

vocabulary.definition: adj. 现代的, 当今的, 现时的
vocabulary.context: ...recognition in the sciences - Bryn Mawr College made the list, even though that list did not account for women's historical and present-day barriers to success in STEM fields.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...科学领域的认可 - 布林莫尔学院 (Bryn Mawr College) 榜上有名，尽管该榜单并未考虑到女性在 STEM 领域取得成功的历史和当今障碍

vocabulary.definition: n. 固执, 坚持不懈, 持续(性), 存留(状态)；[电] 持久
vocabulary.context: Research indicates that these are crucial to creating a sense of belonging, particularly in STEM fields, which in turn predicts greater confidence in one's abilities and greater persistence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究表明，这些对于创造归属感至关重要，特别是在 STEM 领域，这反过来又预示着对自己能力的更大信心和更大的毅力

vocabulary.definition: n. 结合体, 结合, 联合；[经] 联合, 联盟
vocabulary.context: One compelling answer to that question comes from a 2012 Women's College Coalition survey showing that women's college graduates earn advanced degrees at a higher rate than other graduates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这个问题的一个令人信服的答案来自女子大学联盟 2012 年的一项调查，该调查显示女子大学毕业生获得高级学位的比例高于其他毕业生

vocabulary.definition: n. 自由主义者；adj. 慷慨的, 不拘泥的, 宽大的, 自由主义的
vocabulary.context: Specifically, 51 percent of the surveyed women's college graduates earned advanced degrees, compared to 33 percent of liberal arts and 27 percent of flagship public university graduates.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 具体而言，接受调查的女子大学毕业生中有 51% 获得了高级学位，而文科毕业生的这一比例为 33%，旗舰公立大学毕业生的这一比例为 27%

vocabulary.definition: n. 市郊化, 郊区化；近郊化
vocabulary.context: Despite several concerted efforts by city governments to lure residents, suburbanization continues largely unabated.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管市政府做出了一些共同努力来吸引居民，但郊区化仍然基本没有减弱

vocabulary.definition: adj. 一千年的, 千福年的
vocabulary.context: The millennial population is growing fastest in metro areas in the Sun Belt and western states, and slowest in the Snow Belt.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 阳光地带和西部各州的都市区千禧一代人口增长最快，雪带地区增长最慢

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不衰退的, 不减弱的
vocabulary.context: Despite several concerted efforts by city governments to lure residents, suburbanization continues largely unabated.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管市政府做出了一些共同努力来吸引居民，但郊区化仍然基本没有减弱

vocabulary.definition: n. 往来无定的临时工
vocabulary.context: But it will also bring a new set of opportunities for millennials, who are predicted to overtake baby boomers by next year as the largest generation in the country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但这也将为千禧一代带来一系列新的机会，预计他们将在明年超过婴儿潮一代，成为美国人数最多的一代

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 赶上, 突然降临于, 压倒, 代替
vocabulary.context: But it will also bring a new set of opportunities for millennials, who are predicted to overtake baby boomers by next year as the largest generation in the country.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但这也将为千禧一代带来一系列新的机会，预计他们将在明年超过婴儿潮一代，成为美国人数最多的一代

vocabulary.definition: n. 弹回, 反响, 反射；[医] 消退[法], 消肿[法], 浮动诊胎法
vocabulary.context: This ongoing shift towards the suburbs has significant environmental repercussions.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种向郊区的持续转变对环境产生了重大影响

vocabulary.definition: adv. 压倒性地, 不可抵抗地
vocabulary.context: Jobs, too, are overwhelmingly centered around cities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 就业机会也绝大多数集中在城市周围

vocabulary.definition: n. 后退, 凹处, 衰退, 归还；[医] 退缩
vocabulary.context: Will millennials follow older generations to the suburbs as they marry, have children, recover from the shocks of the Great Recession and find affordable housing?
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 千禧一代会跟随老一辈人到郊区结婚、生子、从大衰退的冲击中恢复过来并找到经济适用房吗？

vocabulary.definition: n. 排字；adj. 排字用的；[计] 排字
vocabulary.context: Newspapers also were locked in expensive battles over who could acquire and deploy the latest technologies in typesetting, illustration and printing.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 报纸还陷入了关于谁可以获取和部署最新排版、插图和印刷技术的昂贵斗争

vocabulary.definition: n. 制造, 建造, 虚构的谎言；[医] 虚谈症
vocabulary.context: They sometimes witnessed truly colossal fabrications.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们有时目睹了真正巨大的捏造

vocabulary.definition: adv. 生动地, 活灵活现地, (正式)用图表表示, 以图显示
vocabulary.context: "He speculated graphically on the problems of where the finger came from, whose finger it was, and why the police had not reported a missing man," Blythe wrote.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "他生动地推测了手指来自哪里、是谁的手指以及为什么警方没有报告失踪人员等问题，"布莱斯写道

vocabulary.definition: adj. 拿薪水的, 拿薪金的, 有薪金的, 有薪水的；[经] 有薪金的, 拿薪金的
vocabulary.context: Most of these workers were not salaried employees, so during slow news periods, editors would routinely lay off reporters.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些工人中的大多数都不是受薪雇员，因此在新闻淡季期间，编辑通常会解雇记者

vocabulary.definition: n. 整行铸造排字机；v.i. 以整行排字机排字；v.t. 以整行排字机排
vocabulary.context: For instance, the linotype, a new mechanical typesetting device, cost $3,000.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，linotype，一种新型机械排版设备，售价 3,000 美元

vocabulary.definition: adj. 抢先一步的, 领先的, 占上风的；v.t. 领先, 胜过
vocabulary.context: Back then, the media market was oversaturated; revenues were down; pay was poor; and publishers were locked in circulation battles, working to one-up one another for more subscribers - even if it meant engaging in some unsavory practices.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当时，媒体市场已经过度饱和；收入下降；工资很低；出版商陷入了发行量争夺战，为了获得更多订户而相互竞争--即使这意味着要采取一些令人厌恶的做法

vocabulary.definition: n. 附加物, 附器；[医] 附件, 附器

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 随后，当地水道出现了更多的附属物，更多的故事也随之而来，直到当局展开调查

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 战斗（duke的现在分词形式）
vocabulary.context: Even some tiny communities had more than one daily newspaper duking it out.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 甚至一些小社区也有不止一份日报在竞争

vocabulary.definition: n. 新手, 初学者
vocabulary.context: To cut labor costs, publishers hired novice reporters, but didn't train them.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了削减劳动力成本，出版商雇佣了新手记者，但没有对他们进行培训

vocabulary.definition: n. 合计, 总计, 聚集体；adj. 合计的, 聚集的；v. 聚集, 集合, 合计达；[计] 聚合体; 聚集
vocabulary.context: News executives did start hiring more women than they had in the past, but the aggregate industry data from the census reports show that these women earned about $312 a year in 1900 - half as much as men.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 新闻主管确实开始雇用比过去更多的女性，但人口普查报告中的行业汇总数据显示，1900 年这些女性的年收入约为 312 美元，是男性的一半

vocabulary.definition: n. 忏悔, 临终忏悔, 招认, 承认；[法] 忏悔, 赦免, 临终忏悔
vocabulary.context: Although many colleges and universities have embraced diversity initiatives, disability still gets short shrift.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管许多学院和大学都采取了多元化举措，但残疾问题仍然受到忽视

vocabulary.definition: n. 笨拙, 不熟练, 不灵活, 不好使, 麻烦, 使用不便, 难处理, 尴尬, 棘手, 难应付
vocabulary.context: Awkwardness and avoidance can continue long after college.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尴尬和回避可能会在大学毕业后持续很长时间

vocabulary.definition: n. 易接近, 可进入；[化] 可及性; 可达性; 可接近性; 吸湿性
vocabulary.context: It also stems from the fact that many colleges and university programs "focus mostly on academic and physical accessibility." The social participation of students with disabilities gets less attention.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这还源于许多学院和大学的项目"主要关注学术和身体便利性"这一事实。残疾学生的社会参与较少受到关注

vocabulary.definition: adj. 更远的, 此外的, 更多的；v.t. 促进, 增进, 助长；adv. 更进一步地, 更远地, 此外
vocabulary.context: While rhetoric might seem harmless, this kind of speech furthers the idea that people with disabilities are incapable of success.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然言辞看似无害，但这种言论进一步加深了残疾人无法成功的观念

vocabulary.definition: n. 避免, 避开, 逃避；[经] 回避, 废止, 宣告无效
vocabulary.context: Awkwardness and avoidance can continue long after college.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尴尬和回避可能会在大学毕业后持续很长时间

vocabulary.definition: adv. 数位

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该项目旨在以数字方式绘制校园周围"出现的物理和社会障碍"

vocabulary.definition: n. 性征, 性行为, 性欲；[医] 性别, 性欲
vocabulary.context: While educators might teach about topics like race, class, gender, or sexuality, disability is often left out of the discussion.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 虽然教育工作者可能会教授种族、阶级、性别或性等话题，但残疾往往被排除在讨论之外

vocabulary.definition: adj. 引人怜悯的, 可怜而又可鄙的, 可怜的, 令人怜悯的
vocabulary.context: When people do talk about disability, they may default to "disability rhetoric." This sort of rhetoric casts people with disabilities as either inspirational or pitiable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当人们谈论残疾时，他们可能会默认使用"残疾言论"。这种言论将残疾人视为鼓舞人心或可怜的人

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 下狱, 监禁, 禁闭；[法] 监禁, 使下狱, 禁闭
vocabulary.context: The country incarcerates more people and at a higher rate than any other nation in the world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该国监禁的人数和比率比世界上任何其他国家都高

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生物医学
vocabulary.context: While incarcerated, I applied to six biomedical graduate programs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在监禁期间，我申请了六个生物医学研究生项目

vocabulary.definition: n. 看门人
vocabulary.context: It made me feel like I was nothing more than a criminal in the eyes of the college gatekeepers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这让我觉得自己在大学看门人眼中只不过是一个罪犯

vocabulary.definition: n. 过去, 昔时( past的复数形式 ); 往事; 过去的生活[经历]
vocabulary.context: Beginning in 2019, the Common Application - an online form that enables students to apply to the 800 or so colleges that use it - will no longer ask students about their criminal pasts.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 从 2019 年开始，通用申请表（一种在线表格，使学生能够向使用该表格的大约 800 所大学进行申请）将不再询问学生的犯罪记录

vocabulary.definition: adj. 世界著名的; 名满天下
vocabulary.context: As a formerly incarcerated person who now is an endocrinologist and professor at two world-renowned medical institutions - Johns Hopkins Medicine and Howard University College of Medicine - I believe this move is a positive one.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为一名曾经被监禁的人，现在是约翰霍普金斯大学医学院和霍华德大学医学院两所世界著名医疗机构的内分泌学家和教授，我相信这一举动是积极的

vocabulary.definition: adv. 悲伤地；不幸地；使人痛苦地
vocabulary.context: Despite these facts, education is woefully lacking among those being held in America's jails and prisons.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管有这些事实，美国看守所和监狱中的囚犯仍然严重缺乏教育

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有关处罚的, 刑的, 刑罚的, 刑法上的, (课税)繁重的；[法] 刑事的, 刑法的, 受刑罚的
vocabulary.context: Education provides opportunities for people with criminal records to move beyond their experience with the penal system and reach their full potential.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 教育为有犯罪记录的人提供了超越刑罚系统经验并充分发挥潜力的机会

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 取得资格, 有资格；v.t. 使有资格, 使合格, 限定, 限制, 准予
vocabulary.context: I finished near the top of my graduate school class, suggesting that I was likely qualified for the programs that rejected me.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我在研究生院的成绩接近名列前茅，这表明我很可能有资格参加那些拒绝我的项目

vocabulary.definition: n. 富人；富国
vocabulary.context: been comfortable with very good schools for the haves and less than good ones for the have-nots.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为富人提供非常好的学校，为穷人提供较差的学校

vocabulary.definition: n. 适任, 合格；[法] 合格, 合格性
vocabulary.context: On the federal level, Pell awards should be increased and eligibility expanded for students with the greatest need.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在联邦层面，应该增加佩尔奖，并扩大最需要学生的资格

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 驱逐, 使降低地位, 把...归类, 把...委托给；[法] 流放, 判流放刑, 驱逐
vocabulary.context: Talent and ability are not relegated to those of higher means.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 才华和能力并不属于财力较高的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 信任状, 国书, 文件, 身份证明, 证书；[法] 信用证, 国书, 证书
vocabulary.context: Colleges and universities should also create postsecondary certificates and credentials meeting the needs of students entering careers that do not require college degrees.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 学院和大学还应创建高等教育证书和证书，以满足学生进入不需要大学学位的职业的需求

vocabulary.definition: adj. 公平的, 公正的；[法] 公平的, 公正的, 平衡法的
vocabulary.context: These include building an equitable K-12 educational pipeline that provides better college readiness for all of America's students.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中包括建立公平的 K-12 教育渠道，为所有美国学生提供更好的大学准备

vocabulary.definition: n. 预备, 准备, 敏捷
vocabulary.context: These include building an equitable K-12 educational pipeline that provides better college readiness for all of America's students.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中包括建立公平的 K-12 教育渠道，为所有美国学生提供更好的大学准备

vocabulary.definition: n. 完成；[经] 完井, 结束, 完工
vocabulary.context: Time to degree completion has increased over the past decades due to a number of factors, such as the need to work and inadequate pre-collegiate schooling.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 过去几十年来，由于工作需要和大学预科教育不足等多种因素，完成学位的时间有所增加

vocabulary.definition: n. 可动性, 流动性, 机动性；[化] 迁移率
vocabulary.context: A 2017 study has ranked the university 6th in the nation for social mobility, whereby students from the lower 40 percent of the U.S.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 2017 年的一项研究将这所大学的社会流动性排名全美第六，学生来自美国 40% 的贫困人口

vocabulary.definition: n. 十年, 十
vocabulary.context: Time to degree completion has increased over the past decades due to a number of factors, such as the need to work and inadequate pre-collegiate schooling.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 过去几十年来，由于工作需要和大学预科教育不足等多种因素，完成学位的时间有所增加

vocabulary.definition: n. 协调；调音；调和
vocabulary.context: Love-kindness, affection, sensitive attunement, respect, companionship-is not only difficult to find, but is even more challenging for many people to accept and tolerate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 爱--仁慈、感情、敏感的协调、尊重、陪伴--不仅很难找到，而且对许多人来说更难以接受和容忍

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不足的, 不充分的, 有缺陷的
vocabulary.context: This idealization process is inextricably tied to maintaining an image of oneself as bad or deficient.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种理想化过程与维持自己糟糕或有缺陷的形象密不可分

vocabulary.definition: n. 发生作用之人或物, 工作之人或物；adj. 动作的
vocabulary.context: When the guilt of surpassing one's parent or associate is operant, people fear retaliation and tend to limit or go against their own development.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当超越父母或同事的内疚感产生作用时，人们害怕遭到报复，并倾向于限制或阻碍自己的发展

vocabulary.definition: n. 理想化, 理想化事物；[医] 观念化, 理想化
vocabulary.context: This idealization process is inextricably tied to maintaining an image of oneself as bad or deficient.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种理想化过程与维持自己糟糕或有缺陷的形象密不可分

vocabulary.definition: n. 事实, 现实, 现状
vocabulary.context: In actuality, they attempt to recreate the world they lived in as children to maintain psychological equilibrium.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，他们试图重建自己儿时生活的世界，以维持心理平衡

vocabulary.definition: v.i. 恢复, 复归, 回复, 还原；v.t. 使回复原状, 使回转；n. 恢复原信仰的人；[计] 还原
vocabulary.context: In addition, as love relationships become more meaningful, deep and threatening, people tend to revert to utilizing the same defense mechanisms that their parents used to avoid pain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，随着爱情关系变得更加有意义、深刻和具有威胁性，人们往往会重新利用父母用来避免痛苦的相同防御机制

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使理想化；v.i. 理想化, 形成思想, 描述理想
vocabulary.context: In the developmental process, children idealize their parents at their own expense as part of a psychological survival mechanism.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在成长过程中，孩子会以自己的代价理想化父母，作为心理生存机制的一部分

vocabulary.definition: adj. 似非而是的, 矛盾的, 诡论的；[医] 奇异的, 矛盾的, 逆理的, 反常的
vocabulary.context: Indeed, this paradoxical reaction is largely an unconscious process.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，这种矛盾的反应很大程度上是一个无意识的过程

vocabulary.definition: n. 无法逃避之事, 不可逃避, 必然性；[法] 不能避免
vocabulary.context: If we embrace life and love, we must also face death's inevitability." In particular, the experience of being loved makes one place more value on one's life, and the anticipation of its ending becomes tortuous.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 如果我们拥抱生命和爱，我们也必须面对死亡的不可避免。"特别是，被爱的经历让一个人更加珍惜生命，对生命结局的期待变得曲折

vocabulary.definition: n. 闯入, 侵扰；[医] [向内]突入
vocabulary.context: However painful it may be, people are somehow willing to accept failure or rejection, because these are harmonious with the incorporated negative view of themselves, whereas the intrusion of being loved or having positive responses directed toward them is disruptive of their psychological equilibrium.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 无论多么痛苦，人们都愿意接受失败或拒绝，因为这些与自身的负面看法是和谐的，而被爱的侵入或针对他们的积极反应会破坏他们的心理平衡

vocabulary.definition: n. 浮舟, 浮筒, 浮码头；v.t. 架浮桥于
vocabulary.context: They were on pontoons.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他们在浮桥上

vocabulary.definition: adj. 两人的; 限两人用的; 两人管理的; 有两位艺术家作品的
vocabulary.context: It was one of them two-man subs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这是其中之一两人潜艇

vocabulary.definition: n. 坦克手, 油船, 空中加油飞机, 运油飞机, 油槽/水槽汽车；[化] 槽车; 油槽船
vocabulary.context: But it was a tanker.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但那是一艘油轮

vocabulary.definition: n. 发薪日, 发工资日, 交割日, 过户结帐日；[法] 发薪日
vocabulary.context: And each man, there were twenty of them, would pay him a dollar apiece on payday, which was twice a month.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 每个人，有二十个人，会在发薪日付给他每人一美元，每月两次

vocabulary.definition: n. 鱼雷, 水雷, 地雷；v.t. 用鱼雷袭击, 破坏
vocabulary.context: One torpedo didn't hit the hub, it hit the rudder.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一枚鱼雷没有击中轮毂，而是击中了方向舵

vocabulary.definition: n. 破坏者, 消灭者, 起破坏作用的东西, 驱逐舰
vocabulary.context: Now early that morning, 'course I didn't hear this until later, a destroyer out there started fighting with a sub, and they sank it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那天早上，我当然没有听到这个消息，直到后来，一艘驱逐舰开始与一艘潜艇交战，他们击沉了它

vocabulary.definition: n. 锚；v.t. 抛锚停泊, 使固定；[计] 锚
vocabulary.context: So, they were fighting the fire on the water and the ship, and they finally got the anchor chain around where we tied up, so it stayed upright.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所以，他们在水面和船上救火，最后他们在我们绑住的地方找到了锚链，所以它保持直立

vocabulary.definition: n. 海峡, 航道, 频道；v.t. 引导, 在...上挖沟, 形成河道；[计] 信道, 通道
vocabulary.context: She run in the ground about two or three hundred yards from where we were, to keep it from blocking the channel.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她在距离我们大约两三百码的地方跑，以防止它堵塞通道

vocabulary.definition: n. 同化, 归化, 国有化；[经] 国有化
vocabulary.context: Long-standing commercial ties, private institutions, banks, insurance companies, and shipping companies disappeared, through loss of capital, absorption through nationalization, or by simple destruction.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 长期存在的商业联系、私人机构、银行、保险公司和航运公司因资本损失、通过国有化吸收或简单破坏而消失

vocabulary.definition: adj. 缓和的, 减轻的, 掩饰的；n. 辩解, 缓和物
vocabulary.context: Any assistance that this Government may render in the future should provide a cure rather than a mere palliative.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 本届政府将来可能提供的任何援助都应该提供治疗方法，而不仅仅是治标不治本

vocabulary.definition: n. 脱臼, 转位, 混乱；[化] 位错; 转位
vocabulary.context: In considering the requirements for the rehabilitation of Europe, the physical loss of life, the visible destruction of cities, factories, mines and railroads was correctly estimated but it has become obvious during recent months that this visible destruction was probably less serious than the dislocation of the entire fabric of European economy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在考虑欧洲复兴的要求时，对生命的实际损失以及对城市、工厂、矿山和铁路的明显破坏的估计是正确的，但近几个月来很明显，这种明显的破坏可能没有欧洲经济整个结构的混乱那么严重

vocabulary.definition: n. 评价, 估价
vocabulary.context: I think one difficulty is that the problem is one of such enormous complexity that the very mass of facts presented to the public by press and radio make it exceedingly difficult for the man in the street to reach a clear appraisement of the situation.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我认为一个困难在于，这个问题非常复杂，以至于媒体和广播向公众提供的大量事实使得街上的人很难对局势做出清晰的评估

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有效的, 灵验的
vocabulary.context: It would be neither fitting nor efficacious for this Government to undertake to draw up unilaterally a program designed to place Europe on its feet economically.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 本届政府单方面制定一项旨在使欧洲在经济上站稳脚跟的计划既不合适，也不有效

vocabulary.definition: v. 预示；bide的过去式
vocabulary.context: Thus a very serious situation is rapidly developing which bodes no good for the world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，一种非常严重的局势正在迅速发展，这对世界来说不是什么好兆头

vocabulary.definition: adv. 从那里, 从此
vocabulary.context: Furthermore, governments, political parties, or groups which seek to perpetuate human misery in order to profit therefrom politically or otherwise will encounter the opposition of the United States.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，寻求使人类苦难永久化以从中政治或其他方面获利的政府、政党或团体将遭到美国的反对

vocabulary.definition: adj. 智力上迟钝的
vocabulary.context: Recovery has been seriously retarded by the fact that two years after the close of hostilities a peace settlement with Germany and Austria has not been agreed upon.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 敌对行动结束两年后，尚未与德国和奥地利达成和平解决方案，这一事实严重阻碍了复苏

vocabulary.definition: n. 随后发生的事情, 结果；adj. 作为结果的, 合乎逻辑的
vocabulary.context: Furthermore, the people of this country are distant from the troubled areas of the earth and it is hard for them to comprehend the plight and consequent reactions of the long-suffering peoples, and the effect of those reactions on their governments in connection with our efforts to promote peace in the world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 此外，这个国家的人民远离地球上的动乱地区，他们很难理解长期受苦人民的困境和随之而来的反应，以及这些反应对他们的政府对我们促进世界和平的努力的影响

vocabulary.definition: n. 远见, 深谋远虑；[法] 先见, 预见, 预见的能力
vocabulary.context: With foresight, and a willingness on the part of our people to face up to the vast responsibility which history has clearly placed upon our country, the difficulties I have outlined can and will be overcome.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 只要我们的人民有远见，并愿意承担历史明确赋予我国的巨大责任，我所概述的困难就能够而且一定会被克服

vocabulary.definition: n. 坠落, 沉淀, 降水, 轻率, 急速, 猛冲, 突如其来；[化] 析出
vocabulary.context: As a result, the rapid deforestation exacerbated an already severe drought - in the simulation, deforestation reduced precipitation by five to 15 percent and was responsible for 60 percent of the total drying that occurred over the course of a century as the Mayan civilization collapsed.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 结果，快速的森林砍伐加剧了本已严重的干旱--在模拟中，森林砍伐使降水量减少了 5% 到 15%，并且造成了玛雅文明崩溃一个世纪以来发生的总干旱的 60%

vocabulary.definition: n. 外行, 非专业人员, 未担任神职的一般信徒
vocabulary.context: Scholars and laypeople have proposed countless theories accounting for the collapse, ranging from the plausible (overhunting, foreign invasion, peasant revolt) to the absurd (alien invasion, supernatural forces).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 学者和外行人提出了无数解释崩溃的理论，从合理的（过度狩猎、外国入侵、农民起义）到荒谬的（外星人入侵、超自然力量）

vocabulary.definition: n. 都市风景
vocabulary.context: Interestingly, they also required massive amounts of wood to fuel the fires that cooked the lime plaster for their elaborate constructions - experts estimate it would have taken 20 trees to produce a single square meter of cityscape.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 有趣的是，他们还需要大量的木材来生火，为他们精心制作的建筑烧熟石灰灰泥--专家估计，需要 20 棵树才能形成一平方米的城市景观

vocabulary.definition: n. 干旱, 不毛, 乏味
vocabulary.context: The collapse is especially intriguing because it seemingly occurred at "a time in which developed a sophisticated understanding of their environment, built and sustained intensive production and water systems and withstood at least two long-term episodes of aridity," says B.L.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这次塌陷尤其令人感兴趣，因为它似乎发生在"对环境有了深入了解、建立并维持集约化生产和供水系统、并经受住了至少两次长期干旱的时期"，B.L.

vocabulary.definition: n. 消耗, 耗尽, 放血；[医] 排除, 缺失(液体), 衰竭[状态](由于缺液)
vocabulary.context: The lack of forest cover also contributed to erosion and soil depletion.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 森林覆盖率的缺乏也导致了侵蚀和土壤耗竭

vocabulary.definition: adj. 海基的
vocabulary.context: Coincidentally, trade shifted from overland routes, which crossed the heart of the lowland, to sea-based voyages, moving around the perimeter of the peninsula.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 巧合的是，贸易从穿越低地中心的陆路路线转向围绕半岛周边的海上航行

vocabulary.definition: adj. 欠谨慎的, 没脑筋的
vocabulary.context: In his 2005 book Collapse , though, Jared Diamond put forth a different sort of theory - that a prolonged drought, exacerbated by ill-advised deforestation, forced Mayan populations to abandon their cities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 不过，贾里德·戴蒙德 (Jared Diamond) 在其 2005 年出版的《崩溃》一书中提出了一种不同的理论--长期干旱，加上不明智的森林砍伐，加剧了这种情况，迫使玛雅人放弃了他们的城市

vocabulary.definition: n. 剩余额（surplus的复数形式）
vocabulary.context: Since the traditional elite relied largely upon this trade - along with annual crop surpluses - to build wealth, they were sapped of much of their power.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 由于传统精英主要依靠这种贸易以及每年的农作物盈余来积累财富，因此他们的权力被削弱了

vocabulary.definition: adj. 数量的, 定量的；[医] 定量的, 数量的
vocabulary.context: The other study, published by researchers from Columbia University and elsewhere this week in Geophysical Research Letters , applied quantitative data to these trends.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 哥伦比亚大学和其他地方的研究人员本周在《地球物理研究快报》上发表的另一项研究将定量数据应用于这些趋势

vocabulary.definition: adv. 反常地; 荒谬地; 荒谬可笑地; 不合理地
vocabulary.context: The ultimate humiliation took place in 800, when the leader of the Frankish barbarians in the West, Charlemagne, preposterously claimed that he, and not the ruler in Constantinople, was the Christian emperor.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最终的耻辱发生在公元800年，当时西方法兰克野蛮人的领袖查理曼荒谬地声称，他而不是君士坦丁堡的统治者才是基督教皇帝

vocabulary.definition: adv. 永久地
vocabulary.context: Christian in nature, it was perennially at war with Muslims.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 本质上是基督教，但它长期与穆斯林交战

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 再征服, 夺回
vocabulary.context: This plan largely succeeded: the wealthy provinces in Italy and Africa were reconquered, Libya was rejuvenated, and money bought sufficient diplomatic influence in the realms of the Frankish rulers in Gaul and the Visigoth dynasty in Spain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这一计划基本上取得了成功：意大利和非洲的富裕省份被重新征服，利比亚重新焕发活力，金钱在高卢的法兰克统治者和西班牙的西哥特王朝的领土上购买了足够的外交影响力

vocabulary.definition: n. 编辑, 编纂, 编辑(编纂)物；[计] 编译
vocabulary.context: The Code of Justinian, a compilation of all the imperial laws, was published in 529; soon the Institutions (a handbook) and the Digests (fifty books of jurisprudence) were added.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 查士丁尼法典是所有帝国法律的汇编，于529年出版；很快，《机构》（一本手册）和《文摘》（五十本法理学书籍）又被添加进来

vocabulary.definition: n. 皇后, 女皇
vocabulary.context: However, the image of a luxury-addicted, conspiring, decadent court with treacherous empresses and an inert state system is historically inaccurate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，一个沉迷于奢侈、阴谋诡计、颓废的宫廷、奸诈的皇后和惰性的国家体系的形象在历史上是不准确的

vocabulary.definition: n. 接收, 接任, 接管

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 奥斯曼帝国接管后，许多拜占庭艺术家和学者逃往西方，并带走了珍贵的手稿

vocabulary.definition: n. 杀人者, 凶手, 屠宰者；[法] 杀人者, 凶手
vocabulary.context: The victorious emperor, Basilius II, was surnamed Boulgaroktonos, "slayer of Bulgars." The northern border was now finally secured and the empire flourished.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 胜利的皇帝巴西利乌斯二世的姓氏是布尔加罗克托诺斯（Boulgaroktonos），意为"保加利亚人的杀手"。北部边境现在终于安全了，帝国也繁荣起来

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 给…加姓（surname的过去式与过去分词形式）
vocabulary.context: The victorious emperor, Basilius II, was surnamed Boulgaroktonos, "slayer of Bulgars." The northern border was now finally secured and the empire flourished.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 胜利的皇帝巴西利乌斯二世的姓氏是布尔加罗克托诺斯（Boulgaroktonos），意为"保加利亚人的杀手"。北部边境现在终于安全了，帝国也繁荣起来

vocabulary.context: After the Great Schism of 1054, the eastern (Orthodox) church separated from the western (Roman Catholic) church.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1054年大分裂后，东方（东正教）教会与西方（罗马天主教）教会分离

vocabulary.context: However, the image of a luxury-addicted, conspiring, decadent court with treacherous empresses and an inert state system is historically inaccurate.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，一个沉迷于奢侈、阴谋诡计、颓废的宫廷、奸诈的皇后和惰性的国家体系的形象在历史上是不准确的

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 预言, 预告, 预示
vocabulary.context: Careful observers may foretell the hour
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 细心的观察者可以预测时间

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 戴着耳环的雌性飞向人群中的商店，

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一片紫貂云横亘着威尔金的投掷，

vocabulary.context: Such is that sprinkling which some careless quean
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这就是一些粗心的奎恩所洒的水

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一场即将到来的阵雨预示着你的鸡眼即将开枪，

vocabulary.definition: n. 不速之客, 揩油的人
vocabulary.context: As I say, we're right in the middle of town, and if you stand at our front doors you can see two banks and the Congregational church and the newspaper store and three real-estate offices and about 27 old freeloaders tearing up Central Street because the sewer broke again.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如我所说，我们就在市中心，如果你站在我们的前门，你可以看到两家银行、公理会教堂、报纸商店和三个房地产办事处，以及大约 27 个老贪图便宜的人，因为下水道再次破裂，他们正在撕毁中央大街

vocabulary.definition: n. 中心立轴, (非正式)中心人物, 首脑, 关键人物, (非正式)主要成分
vocabulary.context: Policy is what the kingpins want.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 政策是大佬想要的

vocabulary.definition: n. 卷曲, 使(头发)卷曲
vocabulary.context: ...those chubby berry-faces, the lips all bunched together under her nose, this one, and a tall one, with black hair that hadn't quite frizzed right, and one of these sunburns right across under the eyes, and a chin that was too long - you know, the kind of girl other girls think is very "striking" and "attractive" but never quite makes it, as...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ......那些胖乎乎的浆果脸，嘴唇在鼻子下挤在一起，这个，还有一个高个子，黑发不太卷曲，眼睛下面有一个晒伤，下巴太长--你知道，那种女孩在其他女孩看来非常"引人注目"和"有吸引力"，但从来没有真正做到这一点，因为......

vocabulary.definition: n. 小块；小节；小瘤；小疖
vocabulary.context: Still with that prim look she lifts a folded dollar bill out of the hollow at the center of her nubbled pink top.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她仍然保持着那种拘谨的表情，从她粉红色上衣中央的空洞中取出一张折叠的美元钞票

vocabulary.definition: n. 小块, 瘤, 结节
vocabulary.context: She had on a kind of dirty-pink - beige maybe, I don't know - bathing suit with a little nubble all over it and, what got me, the straps were down.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她穿着一件脏粉色的--也许是米色的，我不知道--泳衣上到处都是小疙瘩，让我着迷的是，带子掉了下来

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 阐明, 解决, 解开；v.i. 散开

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她有一头被阳光和盐漂白过的橡木色头发，盘成一个散乱的发髻，还有一张古板的脸

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不受怀疑的, 无嫌疑的, 未知的, 意料外的；[法] 未被怀疑的, 无嫌疑的
vocabulary.context: The girls, and who'd blame them, are in a hurry to get out, so I say "I quit" to Lengel quick enough for them to hear, hoping they'll stop and watch me, their unsuspected hero.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那些会责怪她们的女孩们都急着要出去，所以我很快地对伦格尔说"我退出"，让她们能够听到，希望她们能停下来看着我，她们毫无怀疑的英雄

vocabulary.definition: n. 手推车

vocabulary.context: That is never assailed by fears,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 永远不会受到恐惧的袭击，

vocabulary.definition: n. 一瞥, 闪光, 掠过, 辉矿类；v.i. 扫视, 闪光, 掠过, 提到, 略说；v.t. 扫视, 反射, 使掠过
vocabulary.context: And whoso watched their glances meet,
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 谁看着他们的目光相遇，

vocabulary.definition: n. 亲属关系；[法] 亲属关系, 家族关系, 关系
vocabulary.context: To have served in this office is to have felt a very personal sense of kinship with each and every American.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 曾在这个办公室任职，让我与每一个美国人产生了一种非常个人化的亲情感

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使动摇
vocabulary.context: As long as there was such a base, I felt strongly that it was necessary to see the constitutional process through to its conclusion; that to do otherwise would be unfaithful to the spirit of that deliberately difficult process, and a dangerously destabilizing precedent for the future.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 只要有这样的基础，我就强烈地感到有必要将立宪程序贯穿到底；否则的话，将不忠于这一刻意困难的进程的精神，并为未来开创危险的破坏稳定的先例

vocabulary.definition: n. 辩护, 证明无辜, 辩明；[法] 辩明, 辩白, 辩护
vocabulary.context: To continue to fight through the months ahead for my personal vindication would almost totally absorb the time and attention of both the President and the Congress in a period when our entire focus should be on the great issues of peace abroad and prosperity without inflation at home.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在接下来的几个月里，继续为我个人的辩护而奋斗几乎会完全占用总统和国会的时间和注意力，而此时我们的全部注意力应该集中在国外和平和国内繁荣的重大问题上

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不诚实的, 不忠实的；[法] 不正当的, 不信实的, 不正确的
vocabulary.context: As long as there was such a base, I felt strongly that it was necessary to see the constitutional process through to its conclusion; that to do otherwise would be unfaithful to the spirit of that deliberately difficult process, and a dangerously destabilizing precedent for the future.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 只要有这样的基础，我就强烈地感到有必要将立宪程序贯穿到底；否则的话，将不忠于这一刻意困难的进程的精神，并为未来开创危险的破坏稳定的先例

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 重新发现
vocabulary.context: To put the bitterness and divisions of the recent past behind us and to rediscover those shared ideals that lie at the heart of our strength and unity as a great and as a free people.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 将过去的痛苦和分歧抛在脑后，重新发现那些共同的理想，这些理想是我们作为一个伟大的自由人民的力量和团结的核心

vocabulary.definition: adv. 全体一致, 无异议, 一致同意
vocabulary.context: I would have preferred to carry through to the finish whatever the personal agony it would have involved, and my family unanimously urged me to do so.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我宁愿坚持到底，无论这会带来个人的痛苦，我的家人一致敦促我这样做

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 信托, 交托, 委托；[经] 委托
vocabulary.context: I have tried, to the best of my ability, to discharge those duties and meet those responsibilities that were entrusted to me.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我已尽我所能，履行这些职责并履行委托给我的责任

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 保险, 确保；v.i. 投保
vocabulary.context: We must now insure that the one-quarter of the world's people who live in the People's Republic of China will be and remain, not our enemies, but our friends.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们现在必须确保生活在中华人民共和国的世界四分之一的人民将不再是我们的敌人，而是我们的朋友

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 划线于...下；n. (表强调的)下划线；[计] 底线
vocabulary.context: Rwanda and the difficulties we have had with this special tribunal underscore the need for such a court.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 卢旺达和我们在这个特别法庭遇到的困难强调了设立这样一个法庭的必要性

vocabulary.definition: adj. 想得到的, 可想象的, 可理解的
vocabulary.context: Indeed, we owe to all the peoples of the world who are at risk because each bloodletting hastens the next as the value of human life is degraded and violence becomes tolerated, the unimaginable becomes more conceivable.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，我们对世界上所有处于危险之中的人民负有责任，因为随着人类生命价值的降低和暴力的容忍，每一次流血都会加速下一次流血，难以想象的事情变得更加容易想象

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 加快, 促进, 发出；adj. 畅通的, 无阻碍的, 迅速的, 方便的
vocabulary.context: Now that administrative improvements have begun, however, the tribunal should expedite cases through group trials, and fulfill its historic mission.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，行政管理改革已经开始，法庭应该通过集体审判加快案件审理速度，履行其历史使命

vocabulary.definition: adj. 机械化的；呆板的
vocabulary.context: Scholars of these sorts of events say that the killers, armed mostly with machetes and clubs, nonetheless did their work five times as fast as the mechanized gas chambers used by the Nazis.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究此类事件的学者表示，凶手大多手持砍刀和棍棒，但他们的作案速度却是纳粹使用的机械化毒气室的五倍

vocabulary.definition: adj. 谋叛的, 起义的, 澎湃的；n. 起义者, 叛乱者
vocabulary.context: In Rwanda, we must hold accountable all those who may abuse human rights, whether insurgents or soldiers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在卢旺达，我们必须追究所有可能侵犯人权的人的责任，无论是叛乱分子还是士兵

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 取...最大值, 使增加(或扩大)到最大限度；[计] 最大化
vocabulary.context: We owe to all the people in the world our best efforts to organize ourselves so that we can maximize the chances of preventing these events.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了世界上所有人，我们要尽最大努力组织起来，以便最大限度地防止这些事件的发生

vocabulary.definition: n. 批发；adj. 批发的, 大规模的；adv. 大规模, 照批发方式；v.i. 经营批发业, 批发；v.t. 批发
vocabulary.context: All of this was done, clearly, to make it easy for otherwise reluctant people to participate in wholesale slaughter.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 显然，所有这些都是为了让本来不愿意的人们更容易参与批发屠宰活动

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无疑的, 确定的；adv. 无疑地, 确定地
vocabulary.context: And so I say to you, though the road is hard and uncertain, and there are many difficulties ahead, and like every other person who wishes to help, I doubtless will not be able to do everything I would like to do, there are things we can do.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所以我对你们说，虽然道路是艰难和不确定的，前面有很多困难，像所有其他愿意提供帮助的人一样，我无疑无法做我想做的一切，但我们可以做一些事情

vocabulary.definition: adj. 文明的, 有礼的
vocabulary.context: Let us work together as a community of civilized nations to strengthen our ability to prevent and, if necessary, to stop genocide.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 让我们作为一个文明国家共同体共同努力，加强我们预防并在必要时制止种族灭绝的能力

vocabulary.definition: n. 加速度计；[化] 加速度计
vocabulary.context: These include their accelerometer (Ak-sell-ur-AHM-eh-tur) and the rotation-sensing gyroscope.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中包括加速计 (Ak-sell-ur-AHM-eh-tur) 和旋转感应陀螺仪

vocabulary.definition: n. 键击；[计] 按键, 击键
vocabulary.context: Researchers first train the programs to recognize keystrokes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究人员首先训练程序识别击键

vocabulary.definition: adj. 用户界面友好的, 用户容易掌握使用的
vocabulary.context: That's because the types of algorithms that researchers have used to comb through sensor data are getting more advanced and user-friendly all the time, says Mehrnezhad at Newcastle University.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 纽卡斯尔大学的 Mehrnezhad 表示，这是因为研究人员用来梳理传感器数据的算法类型一直在变得更加先进和用户友好

vocabulary.definition: n. 脱机；adj. 单独的, 独立的
vocabulary.context: Some safeguard apps could appear as standalone programs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 一些防护应用程序可能会显示为独立程序

vocabulary.definition: n. synchronization 的缩略词；v. synchronize 的缩略词；[网络] 同步
vocabulary.context: One team, for instance, synced a smartphone mic and portable speaker.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，一个团队同步了智能手机麦克风和便携式扬声器

vocabulary.definition: n. 陀螺仪, 回转仪；[化] 陀螺仪
vocabulary.context: These include their accelerometer (Ak-sell-ur-AHM-eh-tur) and the rotation-sensing gyroscope.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 其中包括加速计 (Ak-sell-ur-AHM-eh-tur) 和旋转感应陀螺仪

vocabulary.definition: n. 交换, 平衡, 交易；[经] 权衡, 物物交换
vocabulary.context: There's always a trade-off between security and convenience, he says.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他说，安全性和便利性之间总是需要权衡

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 推论, 演绎出；[法] 推断, 推出, 演绎
vocabulary.context: But sound frequencies could also help a spying app deduce a speaker's identity.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但声音频率也可以帮助间谍应用程序推断说话者的身份

vocabulary.definition: n. 友谊, 亲切, 亲密
vocabulary.context: These extra protections can come at the cost of user friendliness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些额外的保护可能会以牺牲用户友好性为代价

vocabulary.definition: n. 随员, 套房, (一)组, (一)套, 组曲, 继之而来的事；[电] 程序组
vocabulary.context: That's because they're equipped with a suite of sensors.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那是因为它们配备了一套传感器

vocabulary.definition: n. 不易损坏之物；adj. 不易坏的
vocabulary.context: We found the prison's old communal kitchen with a stockpile of non-perishables and sat at a table next to a decommissioned fireplace.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们找到了监狱的旧公共厨房，里面堆满了不易腐烂的食物，并坐在一个废弃的壁炉旁边的桌子旁

vocabulary.definition: n. 前男友
vocabulary.context: I remembered her talking about an ex-boyfriend when we met in New York, and I wondered if he was more than an ex, if he was who she mourned.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我记得我们在纽约见面时她谈到了一位前男友，我想知道他是否不仅仅是一位前男友，他是否就是她所哀悼的人

vocabulary.definition: n. 滑台, 船台
vocabulary.context: We climbed onto old cranes and swam in a slipway.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我们爬上旧起重机，在船台里游泳

vocabulary.definition: n. 青年招待所, 宿舍, 旅店
vocabulary.context: I promised her a place to stay for the night, no funny business, but she said she had to go back to her hostel.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我答应给她一个过夜的地方，没什么好笑的，但她说她必须回旅馆

vocabulary.definition: n. 风头鹦鹉
vocabulary.context: Frances suggested a place for us to stay on Cockatoo Island.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 弗朗西斯为我们建议了在鹦鹉岛上的一个住宿地点

vocabulary.definition: n. 内地；adj. 内地的；adv. 在内地
vocabulary.context: Growing up, we took trips into the outback.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在成长过程中，我们经常去内陆地区旅行

vocabulary.definition: adj. 快活的, 高兴的, 愉快的；[计] 国际算法语言的朱尔斯文本
vocabulary.context: I'd gone to my favorite bar, Hardy's, because I liked that it was always loud there, and people were jovial, even if I wouldn't be, and drinking alone you could always lose yourself without feeling like you completely lost yourself.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我去了我最喜欢的酒吧 Hardy's，因为我喜欢那里总是很吵闹，人们都很快乐，即使我不会，独自喝酒你总是会迷失自己，但又不会感觉完全迷失了自己

vocabulary.definition: n. 固定(状态), 固定物, 设备；[计] 夹具
vocabulary.context: It was a permanent fixture in my life, or what was left of it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它是我生命中永久的一部分，或者说是我生命中剩下的一部分

vocabulary.definition: adj. 永久的, 不断的, 无期限的, 没完没了的, 四季开花的；[经] 永远的, 终身的, 持续的
vocabulary.context: I sat and watched the city in a perpetual blaze.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我坐在那儿，看着这座城市在永恒的大火中

vocabulary.definition: n. 口号, 标语, 口头禅
vocabulary.context: The sea is a catchword for one of the Soviet Union's most spectacular failures of trying to bend nature's will to the demands of a glorious Soviet future.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 海洋是苏联最引人注目的失败之一的口号，它试图让自然意志屈服于苏联辉煌未来的要求

vocabulary.definition: n. 公共浴室, 澡堂, 更衣室
vocabulary.context: And in preparation for the numbers, he believes, will flock here, Juxaby is building a cafe and bathhouse.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他相信，为了应对大量涌入这里的人，Juxaby 正在建造一家咖啡馆和浴室

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 使渗透, 浸透, 使充满, 使饱和；adj. 浸透的, 饱和度高的, 深颜色的；n. 饱和化合物, 饱和脂肪酸
vocabulary.context: The storm saturate with salt and pesticides partially left over from the exposed seabed swept the land.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 风暴充满了裸露海底残留的部分盐和杀虫剂，席卷了陆地

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 灌溉, 冲洗伤口, 使清新；v.i. 灌溉
vocabulary.context: Soviet officials tapped heavily into the main river supplying the Aral to make the desert bloom and irrigate water-thirsty cotton fields.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 苏联官员大力开发供应咸海的主要河流，使沙漠开花并灌溉耗水的棉田

vocabulary.definition: n. 移民, 移居外国(或外地)；[医] 血细胞渗出
vocabulary.context: The region suffered massive emigration.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 该地区遭受大规模移民

vocabulary.definition: n. 赎金, 赎身, 赎回；v.t. 赎, 赎身, 赎回, 勒索赎金
vocabulary.context: Idenvex Arsena(ph) fishes regularly on top of the newly built dam and earns close to $100 for three days work, a king's ransom in these parts.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: Idenvex Arsena(ph) 定期在新建的水坝顶部捕鱼，三天工作收入接近 100 美元，在这些地区相当于国王的赎金

vocabulary.definition: adj. 生态学的, 主张生态保护的；[法] 生态的, 生态学的
vocabulary.context: STEVE INSKEEP, HOST : Now in the former Soviet Republic of Kazakhstan, government and Western officials have reversed what is considered one of mankind's greatest ecological catastrophes.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 主持人史蒂夫·因斯基普：现在，在前苏联哈萨克斯坦共和国，政府和西方官员已经扭转了被认为是人类最大的生态灾难之一的局面

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无慈悲心的, 残忍的
vocabulary.context: For the moment, he and his colleagues wile away the days under the merciless Kazakh sun, singing folk songs.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 目前，他和同事们在哈萨克斯坦无情的阳光下，唱着民歌打发日子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 较少的, 较小的, 次要的
vocabulary.context: The lesser sea has risen 12 feet, and water has crept back to just a dozen miles from places that long ago forgot the sound of waves.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 小海面已经上升了 12 英尺，海水已经从很久以前忘记海浪声的地方渗回了十几英里处

vocabulary.definition: n. 钍；[化] 钍Th
vocabulary.context: So these scientists employed a different dating technique using radioisotopes of uranium and thorium.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，这些科学家采用了一种不同的测年技术，使用铀和钍的放射性同位素

vocabulary.definition: adj. 污染的, 油污的, 能产生污迹的
vocabulary.context: The walls were the canvasses, and the paintings are bold and clearly not some kind of smeary accident.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 墙壁就是画布，画作很大胆，显然不是某种肮脏的意外

vocabulary.definition: n. 突出部份, 悬垂部份；v.t. 悬于...之上, (危险等)逼近；v.i. 悬垂, 逼近
vocabulary.context: "If you want to see them you walk into the deepest, darkest part of the cave and you actually have to lie down and crawl to the cave wall and look up, and they're actually on the ceiling in little overhangs."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: "如果你想看到它们，你就走进洞穴最深、最黑暗的部分，你实际上必须躺下，爬到洞壁上，然后向上看，它们实际上就在天花板上，有一些小悬垂。"

vocabulary.definition: n. 模版, 蜡纸；v.t. 用蜡纸印, 用模版印刷
vocabulary.context: There are stencils where someone pressed a hand up against the wall and then apparently blew liquid ochre over it.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在一些模板上，有人将一只手按在墙上，然后显然在上面吹了液体赭石

vocabulary.definition: n. 碳酸盐；v.t. 使碳酸盐化, 使充满二氧化碳
vocabulary.context: New tests on the rock and calcium carbonate that formed over parts of the ochre show that they were painted 65,000 years ago.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对部分赭石上形成的岩石和碳酸钙进行的新测试表明，它们是在 65,000 年前绘制的

vocabulary.definition: v. 再创造( recreate的现在分词 ); 再现; 消遣; 娱乐
vocabulary.context: That requires abstract, symbolic thinking - recreating something real, like a pattern or a landscape, with symbols.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这需要抽象的、象征性的思维--用符号重新创造一些真实的东西，比如图案或风景

vocabulary.definition: n. 赭石, 赭色；v.t. 涂赭色于
vocabulary.context: The paint used was red ochre, from soil mixed with water.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 使用的油漆是红赭石，来自与水混合的土壤

vocabulary.definition: adj. 海边的, 海滨的；n. 海滨
vocabulary.context: The shells lay in a seaside cave and date back about 115,000 years - far earlier than the first known sign of modern humans in Spain.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这些贝壳位于海边洞穴中，其历史可以追溯到大约 115,000 年前，远早于西班牙第一个已知的现代人类迹象
